Ancient treasures, historical records, and priceless works of art are often safeguarded within their enclaves, protected not merely for wealth but as proof that their civilizations once flourished. Throughout history, Long-haired Paka have served as diplomats, royal chroniclers, master artisans, and keepers of ancestral memory. Across many cultures, they are respected as living reminders that dignity and identity can endure long after kingdoms crumble into legend.

species.feature_effect: Gain +1 to Influence. Gain advantage on Influence (Bartering) checks when trading goods or services.

species.name: Paka Short Hair

description.physical: Short-haired Paka are sleek feline humanoids built for agility, balance, and fast movement. Their coats are short, smooth, and well-suited for humid coastal climates, commonly appearing in sandy browns, storm grays, blacks, whites, and patterned tabby variations. Lean muscle, sharp reflexes, and expressive features give them an energetic and adventurous appearance. Their movements are quick and confident, whether climbing rigging, navigating crowded docks, or balancing across unstable surfaces at sea. Short-haired Paka often favor practical but stylish clothing suited for maritime life, including weathered coats, sailor wraps, belts filled with tools, and lightweight garments that allow freedom of movement.
description.environment: Short-haired Paka dominate coastlines, island settlements, bustling ports, and major trade hubs connected by sea routes. Their cities are loud and vibrant places filled with crowded markets, harbor taverns, shipyards, and lantern-lit docks alive at all hours. Many communities are built directly into cliffsides, harbors, or sprawling canal systems designed around constant trade and travel. Life among the Short-haired Paka is heavily shaped by the ocean, with fishing, shipping, exploration, and naval culture woven into nearly every aspect of daily life.
description.culture: Short-haired Paka culture values adaptability, quick thinking, curiosity, and the willingness to seize opportunity before it disappears. They are natural explorers and risk-takers who often prefer action over hesitation, diving headfirst into new ventures, discoveries, and adventures. Sailors, navigators, merchants, smugglers, pilots, and explorers hold respected places within their society, and stories of dangerous voyages or impossible escapes are celebrated with enthusiasm. Their communities are highly social and expressive, filled with music, laughter, storytelling, and constant negotiation. While sometimes reckless, Short-haired Paka admire cleverness and courage above rigid tradition, believing fortune favors those willing to move first.
description.lore: Short-haired Paka are renowned across the world as daring sailors and fearless explorers who helped chart dangerous coastlines and connect distant civilizations through trade. Ancient stories tell of legendary captains navigating impossible storms, discovering hidden islands, or outrunning entire naval fleets through sheer skill and audacity. Their adaptability has allowed them to thrive in unstable ports and rapidly changing political climates where other cultures struggle to survive. Though outsiders sometimes view them as impulsive or overly ambitious, few can deny their talent for surviving the unpredictable nature of both the sea and society itself. Wherever ships gather and stories are traded, Short-haired Paka are rarely far behind.

species.feature_effect: Gain +1 Agility. Gain advantage on any checks related to navigation, riding, or piloting.

species.name: Shujaa Ash Mane

description.physical: Ash-Manes are rare leonine humanoids distinguished by their pale silver, ash-gray, or unnaturally dark manes said to mark them as touched by fate. Their bodies are broad and powerful like other great feline warriors, yet they carry themselves with a quieter and more restrained presence. Their eyes often possess unusual coloration or reflective qualities that give them an ancient, watchful appearance. Scars, ritual markings, and weathered features are common among older Ash-Manes, reinforcing their reputation as guardians and survivors of great trials. Their clothing and armor tend to be ceremonial and practical in equal measure, combining layered robes, heavy cloaks, sacred jewelry, and battle-worn equipment tied to ancestral tradition.
description.environment: Ash-Manes are most often found in secluded temples, sacred ruins, isolated mountain sanctuaries, and ancient places tied to prophecy or forgotten history. Many act as guardians over old libraries, celestial observatories, tombs, or hidden shrines preserved far from civilization. Their settlements are quiet and reflective, built around meditation halls, sacred fires, and spaces dedicated to preserving memory and ritual. Ash-Manes rarely gather in large numbers, preferring small enclaves or solitary watchposts where they can fulfill ancient duties passed down through generations.
description.culture: Ash-Mane culture revolves around memory, spiritual responsibility, and preservation of forgotten truths. Unlike their more openly prideful Sun-Mane kin, Ash-Manes value contemplation, restraint, and wisdom earned through hardship. Many become mystics, lorekeepers, guardians, philosophers, or watchers entrusted with protecting sacred knowledge and guiding others through moments of uncertainty. Oral histories, celestial interpretation, and ancestral rites are deeply important within their traditions. Their voices and roars are treated as sacred expressions of lineage and purpose, believed to carry the echoes of those who came before them. Ash-Manes are slow to speak but rarely ignored when they do.
description.lore: Legends claim Ash-Manes are born during celestial events, divine trials, or moments of great imbalance in the world. Their unusual manes are believed to be omens tied to destiny, marking them as chosen protectors of ancient wisdom and sacred places. Throughout history, Ash-Manes have appeared beside collapsing kingdoms, forgotten temples, and world-altering events, often acting as guides, judges, or silent defenders against threats few others fully understand. Their roars are said to echo with the voices of ancestors, chilling enemies with dread while strengthening the resolve of allies. To many cultures, encountering an Ash-Mane is considered both a blessing and a warning, a sign that history itself is watching closely.

species.feature_effect: As a Off-Action, when an ally within melee range is targeted by an Attack, you can reduce the attacker's roll by -3.

species.name: Shujaa Sun Mane

description.physical: Sun-Manes are powerful leonine humanoids recognized by their striking manes of gold, tawny brown, copper, or flame-red. Their broad frames, muscular builds, and commanding posture give them a natural presence that draws attention wherever they walk. Thick manes are a source of pride among Sun-Manes, often braided, decorated, or carefully maintained to reflect status, achievements, or lineage. Their eyes commonly carry warm amber or fiery tones that reinforce their reputation for intensity and confidence. Sun-Manes move with bold, deliberate strength, projecting authority even in silence. Their armor and clothing favor regal but practical designs, including layered battle cloaks, ceremonial markings, polished metalwork, and symbols tied to pride or military honor.
description.environment: Sun-Manes are commonly found across savannah kingdoms, sunlit plains, fortified cities, and warrior strongholds where leadership and martial tradition are deeply respected. Their settlements are often built around central gathering arenas, great halls, training grounds, and towering monuments dedicated to heroes and ancestors. Communities value visibility and openness, favoring large communal spaces where stories, celebrations, and public ceremonies can unite the pride. Many Sun-Mane territories are highly organized and defensive, maintained through strong leadership and shared responsibility.
description.culture: Sun-Mane culture revolves around leadership, courage, unity, and glory earned through honorable action. They believe strength carries the responsibility to inspire and protect others, and many are raised from a young age to lead by example. Warriors, champions, commanders, and protectors hold respected positions within society, though leadership is expected to come with discipline and accountability rather than tyranny. Songs, oral epics, and public storytelling are central to preserving the deeds of great heroes and fallen protectors. Sun-Manes value confidence and boldness, but cowardice, betrayal, and abuse of power are considered deep disgraces within the pride.
description.lore: Sun-Manes are remembered throughout history as legendary generals, champions, and unifying figures whose presence alone could rally entire armies or fractured communities. Countless stories portray them standing against impossible odds, leading desperate defenses, or inspiring others to acts of courage through sheer force of will. Many cultures view them as living symbols of honor and perseverance, associating their golden manes with warmth, strength, and protection. While celebrated for their martial prowess, Sun-Manes are equally respected for their loyalty to their people and their refusal to abandon those under their care. Across the world, their names are often carried through song long after the warriors themselves have fallen.

species.feature_effect: As a half-action, give a simple command to an ally within short range. The ally moves their turn in initiative order to immediately after yours.

species.name: Armadaran

description.physical: Armadillo beastmen are compact, durable humanoids distinguished by their natural armored shells and reinforced scales that protect much of their bodies. Their thick plating commonly appears in earthy browns, sandy tans, gray stone tones, or muted reds shaped by the environments they inhabit. Strong claws and sturdy limbs make them excellent diggers, climbers, and survivalists capable of navigating rough terrain with ease. Though shorter than many other beastfolk, they possess surprising endurance and resilience. Their most famous trait is the ability to curl into a tightly protected ball and roll at remarkable speeds, allowing them to escape danger, traverse difficult terrain, or strike with unexpected momentum. Clothing among Armadillo communities is usually practical and travel-oriented, designed to work alongside their shell rather than restrict movement.
description.environment: Armadillo beastmen thrive in deserts, grasslands, rocky badlands, and frontier regions where mobility and adaptability are essential for survival. Their communities are often nomadic or semi-mobile, traveling in caravans that move between trade routes, seasonal camps, and hidden shelters. Wagons, portable workshops, reinforced tents, and underground storage burrows are common features of Armadillo settlements. Because of their wandering lifestyle, they are highly skilled at surviving in harsh environments with limited resources, often uncovering hidden water sources, safe routes, and temporary shelter others would overlook.
description.culture: Armadillo culture values resilience, adaptability, cooperation, and practical problem-solving. Their communities place great importance on mobility and self-sufficiency, teaching younger generations how to travel light, repair equipment, and survive in unpredictable conditions. Scouts, messengers, traders, engineers, and survivalists are highly respected roles within their society. Armadillo gatherings are lively and communal despite their constant movement, often centered around shared meals, music, and storytelling beside traveling campfires. Creativity and resourcefulness are deeply admired, especially the ability to solve difficult problems using limited tools or unconventional thinking.
description.lore: Armadillo beastmen are known across many regions as reliable travelers capable of crossing terrain that would stop others completely. Ancient stories tell of Armadillo caravans surviving endless droughts, outrunning invading armies, or delivering vital supplies through dangerous wastelands by relying on ingenuity and endurance alone. Their rolling charge has become legendary both as a method of travel and an unexpected combat tactic capable of scattering enemies caught unprepared. Though rarely conquerors or rulers, Armadillo communities are respected for their persistence, adaptability, and ability to endure hardship without losing their sense of humor or camaraderie. Across the frontier, their arrival often signals news, trade, and the promise that even the harshest roads can still be traveled.

species.feature_effect: As an action, curl into a ball and roll up to half your movement in a straight line, dealing [[1d6]] Impact damage to all creatures in your path. Targets must succeed on a Physical check or be knocked Prone. You also take the prone condition at the end of the action.

species.name: Chippoterian

description.physical: Chippoterian, commonly known as bat folk, are nocturnal humanoids distinguished by their membranous wings, sharp senses, and wide range of bat-like features. Their appearances vary greatly depending on lineage, with some resembling sleek vampire bats, broad-winged fruit bats, or strange deformed-nosed cave dwellers. Fur coloration ranges from black and gray to reddish brown, pale cream, or mottled patterns suited for darkness and camouflage. Large ears, reflective eyes, hooked claws, and flexible wings make them naturally adapted for flight, climbing, and navigating confined spaces. Chippoterian movements are quiet and fluid, often unsettlingly silent even while airborne. Their clothing tends to be lightweight and layered to avoid interfering with flight, favoring cloaks, wraps, utility straps, and dark practical gear suited for travel and stealth.
description.environment: Chippoterian thrive in dark and sheltered environments such as caves, dense forests, underground cities, abandoned ruins, swamps, and damp urban districts. Their settlements are commonly built vertically within cavern systems, cliff faces, massive trees, or sprawling underground structures where they can move freely through air and shadow. Hanging platforms, suspended bridges, and hidden roosting chambers are common features within their communities. Though capable of surviving in nearly any environment, Chippoterian prefer places with limited sunlight, high humidity, and natural concealment that allow them to move unseen and protected from larger threats.
description.culture: Chippoterian culture places great importance on loyalty, survival, and communal trust. Their tribes and roosts are tightly bonded social groups where cooperation and mutual protection are essential. Because many communities historically survived in dangerous or isolated environments, secrecy and caution became deeply ingrained cultural traits. Chippoterian are known for valuing observation, patience, and subtlety over direct confrontation, producing exceptional scouts, rangers, spies, navigators, and hunters. Storytelling often takes the form of whispered oral traditions, echo songs, and symbolic carvings hidden throughout their settlements. Despite their secretive reputation, Chippoterian communities are often warm and protective toward trusted allies and guests once trust is earned.
description.lore: Chippoterian have long been viewed with equal parts fascination and suspicion due to their nocturnal habits, silent flight, and ability to appear almost ghostlike in darkness. Ancient stories describe them as watchers hidden in forgotten places, quietly observing wars, kingdoms, and secrets from the shadows above. Their exceptional hearing, darkvision, and stealth made them legendary spies and scouts throughout history, capable of infiltrating places others considered impossible to reach. Some legends claim certain Chippoterian bloodlines can navigate entirely through sound and instinct alone, even in total darkness. Though misunderstood by many outsiders, Chippoterian are fiercely loyal to their tribes and allies, defending their communities with relentless precision when threatened.

species.feature_effect: As an Action emit a high pitched sound that allows you to sense all creatures within Short range if they have a physical form.
species.sub-fet-effect: You have a fly speed equal to half your movement speed. You can carry up to 2 willing Medium creatures or 1 willing Large creature without penalty.

species.name: Curlbane

description.physical: Pangolin humanoids are quiet, heavily armored beastfolk covered in overlapping scales that protect much of their bodies from harm. Their scales commonly appear in earthy browns, muted golds, dark greens, or stone-gray tones, often carrying a weathered texture shaped by age and environment. Strong claws allow them to dig through dense soil, climb rough surfaces, and defend themselves with surprising ferocity when necessary. Though typically calm and reserved in demeanor, Pangolin possess immense physical resilience, capable of curling into a tightly armored ball that is nearly impossible to penetrate. Their movements are careful and deliberate, reflecting a patient and observant nature. Clothing among Pangolin communities is usually minimal and practical, designed to accommodate their scales while incorporating natural fibers, carved beads, and symbolic markings.
description.environment: Pangolin communities are most commonly hidden deep within remote forests, overgrown valleys, ancient reserves, and protected natural sanctuaries far from expanding civilization. Their settlements are intentionally difficult to find, built beneath dense canopies, inside massive root systems, or within carefully concealed underground chambers. These territories are treated as sacred ecological preserves where wildlife and natural balance are fiercely protected. Pangolin communities often maintain hidden pathways, natural traps, and protective wards designed to discourage outsiders from disturbing vulnerable ecosystems.
description.culture: Pangolin culture revolves around protection, preservation, patience, and harmony with the natural world. They view themselves not as rulers of nature, but as guardians entrusted with preserving endangered lands and forgotten ecosystems. Their scales are often engraved or painted with symbols representing life cycles, migration patterns, ancestral teachings, and the balance between growth and decay. Elders, druids, wardens, and natural historians hold respected positions within society, guiding younger generations through ritual, storytelling, and careful observation of the environment. Pangolin are highly secretive and cautious around outsiders, believing many natural places have been destroyed through greed and carelessness.
description.lore: Pangolin are remembered in ancient stories as hidden protectors who appear only when sacred lands are threatened. Legends claim their ancestors were entrusted by nature itself to guard the world’s most vulnerable places from exploitation and destruction. Throughout history, Pangolin wardens have defended forests, ruins, and endangered creatures against poachers, invaders, and reckless expansion. Their ability to endure incredible punishment while remaining unshaken has made them symbols of resilience and quiet determination. Many cultures believe the markings etched into a Pangolin’s scales contain fragments of ancient wisdom, recording lessons and warnings passed down through generations of guardians who dedicated their lives to protecting the balance of the natural world.

species.feature_effect: As an off-action, curl into a ball to double your armor until the start of your next turn, but you gain the Constrained condition

species.name: Damreth

description.physical: Beavers are stocky humanoid Gliers standing roughly 4 to 5 feet tall, built for endurance, labor, and aquatic life. Their bodies are covered in dense waterproof fur commonly ranging through rich browns, dark umber, and reddish earth tones. Broad shoulders, strong limbs, and thick hands allow them to manipulate heavy materials with surprising precision, while their large front teeth are capable of cutting through wood and softer stone with ease. Beavers are easily recognized by their wide flat tails, which aid in balance, swimming, and communication through loud warning slaps against water. Their smaller rounded ears and dark observant eyes give them a practical and focused appearance. Clothing among Beaver communities favors durable workwear, tool belts, waterproof fabrics, and reinforced gear designed for construction and outdoor labor.
description.environment: Beaver settlements are most commonly found along rivers, lakes, wetlands, and floodplains where water can be used for travel, engineering, and defense. Their communities are highly structured and carefully planned, built around interconnected dams, canals, bridges, and lodge-like homes reinforced with timber and stone. Waterways are treated as the lifeblood of Beaver society, serving as transportation routes, defensive barriers, and sources of energy for mills and machinery. Even small Beaver settlements are known for impressive infrastructure, often transforming difficult terrain into thriving and efficient communities through careful engineering and teamwork.
description.culture: Beaver culture values cooperation, craftsmanship, planning, and steady hard work. They believe strong communities are built through shared effort, and nearly every Beaver is taught construction, repair, and practical problem-solving from a young age. Builders, engineers, architects, and labor organizers hold highly respected positions within society, though even leaders are expected to contribute directly to communal projects. Beaver communities are highly organized and efficient, often dividing labor carefully to ensure stability and long-term growth. Despite their industrious reputation, Beavers also place great importance on family gatherings, communal meals, storytelling, and celebrating the completion of major projects together.
description.lore: Beavers are renowned across the world for their engineering brilliance and ability to construct thriving settlements in places others consider uninhabitable. Ancient stories describe Beaver builders redirecting rivers, constructing massive defensive dams, or rebuilding entire towns after floods and disasters. Their skill at large-scale infrastructure projects has made them invaluable allies to kingdoms, trade networks, and frontier settlements alike. While not typically warriors by nature, Beaver communities are fiercely protective of their homes and capable of turning their engineered environments into nearly impenetrable defenses when threatened. Many cultures view them as symbols of persistence, cooperation, and the idea that civilization itself is built one careful piece at a time.

species.feature_effect: You deal double damage to structures and can make a bite attack as an off-action, dealing [[1d4]] Piercing damage.

species.name: Leporidae

description.physical: Leporidae are agile rabbit-like humanoids known for their long ears, powerful legs, and alert expressive features. Their fur ranges across soft whites, browns, grays, blacks, and mottled patterns suited for blending into forests, fields, and underground warrens. Large eyes and constantly shifting ears make them highly perceptive and emotionally expressive, often revealing their mood before they speak. Though lightly built, Leporidae possess remarkable speed, reflexes, and endurance, capable of darting through tight spaces or escaping danger with explosive bursts of movement. Their clothing tends to favor layered travel garments, scarves, patched coats, satchels, and practical gear suited for wandering and survival.
description.environment: Leporidae are most commonly found in underground warrens, hidden hillside villages, overgrown burrows, and small traveling communities spread across the world. Following generations of persecution, many settlements were intentionally built beneath the earth or concealed within dense wilderness to avoid attention from outsiders. Their underground homes are warm, interconnected spaces designed around family, communal living, and hidden escape routes. Despite this caution, Leporidae are still highly mobile people, frequently traveling between towns and settlements as merchants, guides, performers, or wanderers seeking connection and opportunity.
description.culture: Leporidae culture values humility, kindness, resilience, and the importance of companionship. Though shaped by hardship and fear in the past, they refuse to allow bitterness to define their identity. Family and friendship are central to their communities, with gatherings often focused around shared meals, storytelling, music, and celebration of small joys. Many Leporidae develop strong survival instincts and cautious habits, but they are also naturally curious and social, eager to form bonds when trust is earned. Hospitality is treated as sacred within their culture, especially toward travelers or those displaced from home. They believe kindness and laughter are acts of defiance against a cruel world.
description.lore: The Leporidae carry a painful history marked by persecution and superstition. For generations, both Leporidae and halflings were hunted under the false belief that their feet carried supernatural luck. Entire communities were driven underground or scattered across distant regions to survive. This dark chapter deeply shaped their culture, teaching caution and secrecy while strengthening their reliance on one another. Despite this history, Leporidae became known not for vengeance, but for resilience and compassion. Stories across the world often portray them as wandering companions, hidden heroes, or unlikely survivors who continue seeking friendship and love despite the hardships inflicted upon them. To many, the endurance of the Leporidae stands as proof that gentleness can survive even the cruelest of histories.

species.feature_effect: Once per day, when you fail a check, you can reroll it and take the new result.

species.name: Molefolk

description.physical: Molefolk are compact subterranean Gliers with broad shoulders, dense fur, and powerful clawed hands built for digging through earth and stone. Their bodies are sturdy and durable, designed to withstand the constant strain of tunneling and underground labor. Fur coloration commonly appears in dark browns, charcoal grays, black, or muted earth tones that blend naturally with the subterranean environments they inhabit. Many possess reduced eyesight adapted for darkness, relying instead on heightened senses of touch, vibration, and hearing to navigate underground spaces. Molefolk movements are deliberate and efficient, capable of squeezing through narrow passages and excavating tunnels with remarkable speed. Their clothing is practical and reinforced, often consisting of durable fabrics, utility harnesses, lantern gear, and tools worn directly on the body for easy access underground.
description.environment: Molefolk thrive beneath the surface in sprawling tunnel networks, underground cities, mining colonies, and hidden cavern settlements. Their communities are carved directly into the earth, connected through intricate passageways, reinforced chambers, subterranean waterways, and vast excavation systems built over generations. Underground agriculture, fungal gardens, geothermal workshops, and crystal-lit gathering halls are common within Molefolk settlements. They are highly skilled at transforming hostile underground terrain into stable and livable environments, often constructing hidden escape tunnels and defensive routes known only to their people.
description.culture: Molefolk culture values resilience, practicality, cooperation, and craftsmanship. Life underground demands trust and collective effort, leading to tightly knit communities where every member contributes to survival and maintenance of the tunnels. Builders, excavators, miners, engineers, and navigators hold respected positions within society, and younger Molefolk are taught from an early age how to survive cave-ins, navigate tunnels, and work alongside others in dangerous conditions. Their traditions emphasize endurance and quiet reliability rather than glory or status. Storytelling, carving, and preservation of underground maps are deeply important cultural practices, ensuring ancestral knowledge is never lost beneath shifting stone.
description.lore: Molefolk are regarded as master excavators and survivors capable of thriving where most surface dwellers would quickly perish. Ancient stories speak of Molefolk uncovering forgotten ruins, hidden civilizations, and entire worlds buried beneath the earth. Their tunnel systems are said to stretch for unimaginable distances, connecting isolated regions through secret underground passages known only to them. Throughout history, they have served as miners, engineers, guides, and protectors of subterranean trade routes. Though often overlooked by surface cultures, Molefolk quietly shaped the foundations of countless cities and kingdoms from below, proving that strength and civilization are not always built beneath the open sky.

species.feature_effect: You have a burrow speed equal to half your movement and are immune to the Blind condition.

species.name: Oposhka

description.physical: Opossums are slender humanoid Gliers recognized for their flexible bodies, grayish-white fur, pointed snouts, and long prehensile tails capable of supporting their weight while climbing or hanging from branches. Their small rounded ears, dark reflective eyes, and twitching whiskers give them an alert and curious appearance. Opossums are naturally agile climbers with quick hands well-suited for scavenging, trading, and manipulating delicate objects. Though not physically imposing, they are remarkably resilient and adaptable, capable of surviving in difficult environments through cleverness and resourcefulness. Their clothing is often mismatched but practical, commonly layered with pouches, belts, trinkets, and collected curiosities gathered from their travels and trades.
description.environment: Opossums are most commonly found in forested regions, overgrown settlements, swamplands, and trade roads where travel and scavenging opportunities are plentiful. Their homes are typically built high within trees, inside hidden burrows, or woven into interconnected woodland communities designed for climbing and mobility. Opossum settlements often appear cluttered but lively, filled with hanging lanterns, collected treasures, improvised workshops, and market stalls overflowing with unusual goods. They are highly adaptable and capable of integrating into larger cities, though they tend to gather in tightly connected neighborhoods filled with barter, trade, and constant social activity.
description.culture: Opossum culture values curiosity, adaptability, trade, and the preservation of unusual objects and stories. They are natural collectors fascinated by anything shiny, strange, rare, or historically significant, often viewing objects as pieces of living history rather than simple possessions. Merchants, scavengers, tinkers, traders, and collectors hold respected places within their society, and many Opossums spend their lives wandering between settlements searching for rare discoveries. Their communities are highly social and conversational, with stories about trades, bargains, and found treasures passed around as proudly as heroic legends. Though playful and sometimes chaotic, Opossums are surprisingly clever negotiators capable of surviving through wit and cooperation rather than force.
description.lore: Opossums are widely known as wandering collectors and resourceful traders who seem capable of finding value in things others overlook entirely. Ancient tales describe Opossum caravans uncovering forgotten relics, preserving artifacts from collapsed civilizations, or turning worthless scraps into fortunes through sheer ingenuity. Their fascination with shiny and unusual objects has made them invaluable merchants and information brokers across countless regions. While outsiders sometimes dismiss them as scavengers or hoarders, many underestimate the knowledge hidden within their sprawling collections and sharp memories. Across forests, markets, and frontier roads alike, Opossums are remembered as adaptable survivors who can make a home, a deal, or a story out of nearly anything they find.

species.feature_effect: Your prehensile tail is a natural weapon (Agi or Str; Light; 1d6 Impact). On a success+, you may knock the target Prone instead of rolling on the success table.

species.name: Ornithian

description.physical: Platypuses are unusual humanoid Gliers with streamlined bodies, waterproof fur, and distinctive duck-like bills that immediately set them apart from other species. Standing roughly 3 to 4 feet tall, they possess compact but agile builds well-adapted for aquatic life. Their fur commonly appears in rich browns and muted earth tones that help them blend naturally into riverbanks and wetlands. Webbed hands and feet allow for excellent swimming, while their broad tails aid with balance and movement in water. Small rounded ears and dark observant eyes give Platypuses an alert yet unreadable appearance. Their most remarkable trait is their ability to sense faint electrical signals in water, allowing them to navigate murky environments and detect movement with uncanny precision. Clothing among Platypuses tends to be practical and water-resistant, favoring simple wraps, travel gear, and utility-focused equipment.
description.environment: Platypuses are unusual humanoid Gliers with streamlined bodies, waterproof fur, and distinctive duck-like bills that immediately set them apart from other species. Standing roughly 3 to 4 feet tall, they possess compact but agile builds well-adapted for aquatic life. Their fur commonly appears in rich browns and muted earth tones that help them blend naturally into riverbanks and wetlands. Webbed hands and feet allow for excellent swimming, while their broad tails aid with balance and movement in water. Small rounded ears and dark observant eyes give Platypuses an alert yet unreadable appearance. Their most remarkable trait is their ability to sense faint electrical signals in water, allowing them to navigate murky environments and detect movement with uncanny precision. Clothing among Platypuses tends to be practical and water-resistant, favoring simple wraps, travel gear, and utility-focused equipment.
description.culture: Platypus culture is solitary, introspective, and deeply connected to the rhythms of the natural world. They value adaptability, observation, and self-sufficiency over status or expansion. Most Platypuses prefer small family groups or isolated communities rather than large settlements, leading outsiders to view them as mysterious or difficult to understand. Knowledge of waterways, weather patterns, aquatic life, and survival techniques is carefully preserved through oral tradition and practical teaching. While generally peaceful, Platypuses are cautious around outsiders and rarely reveal the full extent of their abilities or settlements to strangers. Curiosity and problem-solving are highly respected traits within their culture, often expressed through experimentation, exploration, and subtle craftsmanship.
description.lore: Platypuses are widely regarded as one of the strangest and most enigmatic of the Gliers, with many ancient stories portraying them as creatures touched by unusual forces of nature. Their ability to sense electrical currents has led some cultures to believe they can perceive hidden truths or navigate places invisible to others. Throughout history, Platypuses have served as elusive guides, river scouts, herbalists, and wanderers capable of surviving in environments where most would quickly fail. Because they rarely involve themselves in politics or large-scale conflict, much about their society remains unknown to outsiders. Those few who earn the trust of a Platypus community often discover quiet but remarkably resourceful people whose understanding of the natural world rivals even the oldest scholars.

species.feature_effect: You can make an unarmed strike against flesh that deals an additional [[1d4]] Toxic damage. You gain advantage on balance checks while swimming or climbing.

species.name: Quillkin

description.physical: Quillkin are sturdy humanoid Gliers covered in dense fur and sharp defensive quills that extend across their backs, shoulders, arms, and tails. Their quills vary in length, coloration, and pattern depending on lineage, commonly appearing in black, white, brown, cream, or striped combinations that naturally warn predators to keep their distance. Beneath their defensive exterior, Quillkin possess compact but powerful builds designed for endurance and survival rather than speed. Their movements are cautious and deliberate, often accompanied by subtle quill rattling when nervous or threatened. Though intimidating in appearance, their expressions are usually calm, reserved, and observant. Clothing among Quillkin is carefully designed to accommodate their quills, favoring layered wraps, open-backed garments, reinforced travel gear, and practical protective materials.
description.environment: Quillkin are most commonly found in dense forests, rocky hills, overgrown wilderness, and isolated frontier regions where natural cover and defensible terrain are abundant. Their homes are often hidden within burrows, hollow trees, cave systems, or heavily fortified woodland shelters built for privacy and protection. Quillkin communities tend to remain small and widely spaced apart, reflecting their naturally cautious and solitary tendencies. They value environments that provide security, concealment, and stable access to food and resources without attracting unnecessary attention.
description.culture: Quillkin culture emphasizes caution, self-reliance, resilience, and respect for personal boundaries. They prefer avoiding unnecessary conflict whenever possible, believing survival often depends on patience and preparation rather than aggression. Many Quillkin become trappers, survivalists, herbalists, scouts, or wardens who specialize in protecting territory and avoiding danger. Their communities are quiet and practical, placing great value on craftsmanship, defensive architecture, and mutual respect. Though reserved around strangers, Quillkin form deep and loyal bonds once trust is earned. Storytelling and teaching often focus on lessons of survival, endurance, and the importance of knowing when to stand firm versus when to retreat.
description.lore: Quillkin are remembered as resilient survivors capable of enduring dangers that would overwhelm less cautious people. Ancient stories often portray them as solitary wanderers, hidden guardians, or underestimated defenders who turned impossible situations in their favor through patience and preparation. Their quills are legendary for deterring predators and attackers alike, with some tales describing warriors retreating in panic after underestimating a cornered Quillkin. Because of their defensive nature, many Quillkin communities survived wars and disasters simply by remaining hidden and refusing to be drawn into conflicts not their own. Across countless regions, they are respected as symbols of perseverance and the quiet strength found in those who endure rather than dominate.

species.feature_effect: When hit by a melee Attack, deal 1 Piercing damage back to the attacker.

species.name: Rakkari

description.physical: Raccoons are compact humanoid Gliers standing roughly 3 to 4 feet tall, recognized by their grayish-brown fur, bushy ringed tails, and distinctive dark markings around the eyes resembling natural masks. Their dexterous hands are remarkably nimble, capable of handling delicate tools, mechanisms, and intricate craftsmanship with ease. Sharp claws, pointed snouts, small rounded ears, and bright observant eyes give them an alert and curious appearance. Though not physically imposing, Raccoons are quick, adaptable, and constantly active, often carrying tools, gadgets, or half-finished inventions wherever they go. Their clothing is practical and heavily customized, commonly layered with belts, pouches, utility harnesses, patched fabrics, and improvised modifications suited for tinkering and exploration.
description.environment: Raccoon communities are commonly found near rivers, forests, bustling cities, industrial districts, and trade hubs where access to materials and opportunity are plentiful. Their settlements are lively and cluttered places filled with workshops, hidden compartments, improvised machinery, and experimental devices in various stages of completion. Many communities are built vertically through interconnected rooftops, scaffolding, tree platforms, or tunnel systems designed for mobility and creativity. Raccoons adapt easily to changing environments and are often among the first to repurpose abandoned structures, salvage materials, or turn ruined spaces into thriving workshops and homes.
description.culture: Raccoon culture revolves around invention, curiosity, collaboration, and creative problem-solving. They are highly social and naturally drawn toward experimentation, often working together to design gadgets, traps, tools, and unconventional solutions to difficult problems. Inventors, engineers, scavengers, trappers, and mechanics hold respected roles within society, though individual creativity is valued just as highly as formal expertise. Failure is rarely viewed as shameful among Raccoons, with mistakes treated as opportunities to learn and improve. Their communities are energetic and chaotic in equal measure, filled with constant conversation, shared projects, and spontaneous ideas that may or may not explode unexpectedly.
description.lore: Raccoons are widely known as inventive tinkerers capable of turning scraps, salvaged materials, and impossible ideas into functional creations. Ancient stories describe Raccoon engineers building ingenious traps, hidden mechanisms, and devices far beyond what outsiders believed possible. Their natural curiosity and willingness to experiment have made them valued allies in construction, exploration, and technological advancement across countless regions. While some cultures stereotype them as troublemakers or scavengers, many underestimate the brilliance hidden beneath their chaotic workshops and playful attitudes. Across cities, forests, and frontier settlements alike, Raccoons are remembered as clever builders who can always find a solution, even if that solution is slightly unstable.

species.feature_effect: Once per day, create a simple gadget or trap from found materials. You also gain advantage on Crafting checks to pick locks or disable traps.

species.name: Sciurian

description.physical: Squirrels are slender humanoid Gliers standing roughly 3 to 4 feet tall, recognized for their reddish-brown fur, large bushy tails, and restless energetic movements. Their sharp claws and lightweight frames make them exceptional climbers capable of navigating trees, rooftops, and narrow ledges with ease. Round expressive eyes and small tufted ears give them an alert and constantly curious appearance, often reflecting excitement, suspicion, or distraction all at once. Their tails serve both for balance and emotional expression, flicking rapidly during moments of stress or enthusiasm. Clothing among Squirrel communities tends to be practical but lively, favoring layered travel gear, satchels, climbing harnesses, scarves, and pouches filled with collected supplies or snacks.
description.environment: Squirrels are most commonly found in dense forests, woodland settlements, overgrown ruins, and elevated communities built high among trees or hidden within massive root systems. Their homes are often connected through rope bridges, platforms, ladders, and hidden pathways that allow quick movement through the canopy. Many also construct extensive burrow systems beneath their settlements for food storage and protection during harsh seasons. Squirrel communities are lively and bustling places filled with hidden caches, communal markets, and constant movement as residents gather supplies, trade goods, and prepare for future scarcity.
description.culture: Squirrel culture revolves around preparation, exploration, curiosity, and communal survival. Though adventurous by nature and eager to explore the unknown, they also possess strong instincts toward gathering and protecting resources for the future. Food hoards are deeply important within their communities, often treated as symbols of responsibility, foresight, and family stability. Travelers, scouts, climbers, couriers, and foragers are highly respected roles among Squirrels, and many spend years wandering before eventually returning home with stories and discoveries. Despite their occasional possessiveness over supplies, Squirrels are highly social and form strong bonds through shared exploration, trade, and communal gatherings.
description.lore: Squirrels are remembered as energetic wanderers and clever survivalists capable of thriving through preparation and adaptability. Ancient stories often portray them discovering hidden routes through dangerous forests, surviving impossible winters through carefully guarded stores, or uncovering forgotten treasures while chasing simple curiosity. Their dual nature as both explorers and hoarders has become central to how other cultures view them: restless adventurers who always seem to know where hidden resources can be found. Though sometimes underestimated due to their size and excitable personalities, Squirrels have repeatedly proven themselves capable of surviving disasters through planning, cooperation, and relentless determination. Across forests and frontier settlements alike, their communities are known as lively places filled with stories, supplies, and the promise of another journey waiting just beyond the trees.

species.feature_effect: In dim light or darkness, you gain advantage on Agility or Stealth checks made to steal or hide objects. Once per Long Rest, you can instantly palm or conceal a small item without a check.

species.name: Camelon

description.physical: Camelon are hardy half-cloven humanoids adapted for survival in some of the harshest environments in the world. Their tall, lean builds are designed for endurance over long distances, with broad feet suited for traversing sand and uneven terrain without sinking. Camelon commonly possess coarse fur in sandy browns, pale creams, sun-bleached whites, and reddish desert tones that help them blend naturally into arid landscapes. Many display elongated facial features, thick eyelashes, and protective nostrils adapted to wind and dust storms. Despite often appearing calm or slow-moving, Camelon possess tremendous stamina and can travel for incredible lengths of time without tiring. Their clothing favors layered robes, wrapped fabrics, protective veils, and lightweight travel gear designed to shield against heat, sand, and harsh weather.
description.environment: Camelon thrive in deserts, dry plains, rocky badlands, and vast trade routes stretching between isolated settlements and oasis cities. Their communities are commonly nomadic or semi-nomadic, traveling alongside caravans, migrating herds, or seasonal water sources. Settlements are often built around oasis hubs, cliffside shelters, or mobile encampments capable of relocating when resources become scarce. Water is treated as sacred within Camelon society, and even the smallest settlements are carefully engineered to preserve and distribute it efficiently. Caravan routes and hidden wells are among their most closely guarded secrets.
description.culture: Camelon culture revolves around endurance, hospitality, trade, and respect for survival in unforgiving environments. They value patience and resourcefulness above aggression, believing survival comes from preparation and cooperation rather than domination. Merchants, navigators, caravan leaders, water keepers, and guides hold highly respected positions within society due to their importance in maintaining trade and travel across dangerous terrain. Hospitality is considered sacred among Camelon communities, as refusing aid to travelers in the desert is often viewed as condemning them to death. Oral traditions, navigation songs, and stories of legendary journeys are central to their culture, preserving knowledge of routes, storms, and ancient settlements lost beneath shifting sands.
description.lore: Camelon are renowned throughout the world as unmatched desert travelers capable of surviving where most others would quickly perish. Ancient stories tell of Camelon caravans crossing endless dunes guided only by stars and memory, carrying trade, knowledge, and diplomacy between civilizations separated by impossible distances. Their resilience and understanding of arid environments made them indispensable as merchants, guides, and negotiators throughout history. Though outsiders sometimes mistake their calm demeanor for weakness, Camelon are fiercely protective of their people, traditions, and sacred water sources. Across deserts and frontier lands alike, they are remembered as enduring wanderers who transformed barren wastelands into living pathways connecting the world together.

species.feature_effect: You require only half the normal amount of food and water to survive. Once per Long Rest, you may ignore one instance of elemental damage.

species.name: Elephantine

description.physical: Elephantine are towering humanoids with thick skin, immense builds, and powerful trunks that serve as both delicate tools and formidable instruments of strength. Their skin commonly appears in shades of gray, brown, slate, or muted earth tones, often marked by age lines, ceremonial paint, or carved adornments that reflect lineage and personal history. Large ears, broad shoulders, and heavy limbs give them an imposing presence, though their movements are usually calm, measured, and deliberate. Their trunks are highly dexterous, capable of fine craftsmanship, intricate gestures, and immense force when necessary. Clothing among Elephantine communities favors layered fabrics, ceremonial jewelry, woven shawls, and practical garments designed for both comfort and cultural symbolism.
description.environment: Elephantine communities are most commonly found in fertile river valleys, savannahs, ancient woodland regions, and sacred territories rich with historical or spiritual importance. Their settlements are spacious and enduring, built from stone, timber, and carefully shaped earthworks designed to last for generations. Great communal halls, memory archives, workshops, and ceremonial plazas often form the heart of their cities. Elephantine architecture is renowned for blending practicality with artistry, creating structures that feel both monumental and harmonious with the surrounding landscape. Sacred sites, ancestral monuments, and preserved natural spaces are treated with immense reverence and carefully protected from destruction.
description.culture: Elephantine culture values memory, wisdom, stewardship, and communal responsibility. Elders and matriarchs hold respected leadership roles, guiding their people through experience, diplomacy, and preservation of tradition rather than force alone. Oral histories, craftsmanship, music, and ceremonial storytelling are central to maintaining cultural identity across generations. Elephantine believe history must be remembered so that mistakes are not repeated, and many dedicate their lives to preserving records, teachings, and ancestral knowledge. Artisans, architects, historians, healers, and environmental caretakers are among the most honored members of society. Though peaceful and compassionate by nature, Elephantine possess fierce protective instincts toward their communities and sacred lands.
description.lore: Elephantine are remembered throughout history as wise builders, keepers of memory, and guardians of ancient traditions. Legends describe them carrying the histories of entire civilizations within their memories, preserving knowledge even after kingdoms and monuments crumbled into ruin. Their role as protectors of sacred places has made them respected mediators and trusted stewards across countless regions. Ancient roads, monuments, aqueducts, and enduring wonders are often attributed to Elephantine craftsmanship, their creations designed not only to function, but to honor the world around them. Though slow to anger, stories warn that when sacred lands or loved ones are threatened, the strength of the Elephantine can become as unstoppable and devastating as a stampede itself.

species.feature_effect: Gain an additional +5 armor that can be healed or repaired. Your trunk functions as both tool and weapon. You can interact with objects, grapple creatures, or make an off-action unarmed Attack that deals [[1d4]] Impact damage.

species.name: Hippokin

description.physical: Hippokin are massive humanoids with broad builds, thick hides, and powerful jaws reminiscent of hippopotamuses. Their skin commonly appears in shades of gray, brown, slate, or muted violet tones, often carrying scars, river markings, or decorative paints tied to family and community traditions. Despite their immense size, Hippokin are graceful and controlled in the water, capable of swimming with surprising speed and precision. Their dense bodies and nearly impenetrable hides make them exceptionally durable, while their heavy frames allow them to exert tremendous force when necessary. Hippokin movements are usually calm and deliberate, giving them an air of patience and stability. Clothing among their people favors lightweight layered fabrics, river-resistant materials, tool harnesses, and practical garments suited for humid environments and water travel.
description.environment: Hippokin thrive in river valleys, marshlands, wetlands, floodplains, and canal cities built around major waterways. Their communities are often sprawling settlements connected through bridges, docks, canals, and floating platforms that allow easy travel by water. Homes and public structures are commonly reinforced against floods and designed to blend naturally with surrounding wetlands. Water is central to nearly every aspect of Hippokin life, serving as transportation, defense, trade, and a source of cultural identity. Many settlements become important regional centers for commerce, craftsmanship, and the exchange of ideas due to their strategic positions along major rivers and trade routes.
description.culture: Hippokin culture values wisdom, patience, innovation, and communal cooperation. They are known for their calm and measured approach to conflict, often preferring diplomacy, planning, and thoughtful discussion over impulsive action. Builders, inventors, engineers, navigators, scholars, and mediators hold highly respected positions within society. Hippokin communities encourage the sharing of knowledge and practical problem-solving, believing progress comes through collaboration and steady refinement rather than reckless ambition. Public forums, invention halls, and trade gatherings are central to their culture, allowing ideas and discoveries to spread freely between communities. Though peaceful by nature, Hippokin believe strength should be used decisively when protection becomes necessary.
description.lore: Hippokin are remembered throughout history as ingenious builders and guardians of the waterways that connect civilizations together. Ancient stories describe them constructing massive canal systems, flood defenses, and river cities that transformed entire regions into thriving centers of trade and learning. Their settlements became hubs where merchants, travelers, and scholars exchanged goods, technologies, and ideas from distant lands. Though often perceived as slow-moving or passive by outsiders, Hippokin warriors are feared for their ability to control aquatic terrain and turn rivers or marshes into deadly battlefields. Legends speak of invading armies vanishing into flooded marshlands after underestimating the patience, strategy, and overwhelming strength of Hippokin defenders.

species.feature_effect: Gain an additional +5 armor that can be healed or repaired. While submerged in water, you have advantage on Physical checks and can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.

species.name: Roxodon

description.physical: Roxodon are massive humanoids with thick armor-like skin, immense physical strength, and powerful horns that make them instantly recognizable. Their hides commonly appear in shades of gray, stone brown, slate, or dusty earth tones, often scarred from years of labor, battle, and survival. Broad shoulders, dense muscle, and heavy frames give them an imposing presence capable of intimidating most threats without a word. Their horns vary greatly in size and shape between bloodlines, often carrying ceremonial carvings, metal reinforcements, or markings tied to personal achievements. Despite their bulk, Roxodon are capable of explosive force when charging, turning their immense momentum into devastating impact. Clothing and armor among Roxodon communities favor reinforced leathers, practical war gear, ceremonial wraps, and durable materials built to withstand harsh conditions.
description.environment: Roxodon are commonly found in rugged plains, rocky highlands, fortified settlements, and frontier territories where resilience and strength are necessary for survival. Their communities are built to endure, featuring thick walls, reinforced gates, training grounds, and communal halls designed around protection and stability. Many settlements are positioned along important trade routes or vulnerable borders where Roxodon guardians can defend nearby regions from monsters, raiders, or invading forces. Their architecture reflects practicality and permanence, emphasizing structures capable of lasting for generations.
description.culture: Roxodon culture values endurance, discipline, protection, and controlled strength. Physical training and martial skill are deeply respected, though power is expected to be guided by wisdom and responsibility rather than aggression alone. Guardians, soldiers, builders, and protectors hold honored positions within society, and younger Roxodon are taught from an early age that true strength exists to defend others. Their communities are known for being surprisingly compassionate and generous despite their intimidating appearance, often offering shelter and aid to those in need. Loyalty, patience, and reliability are treated as core virtues, while cowardice and cruelty are viewed as failures of character rather than weakness of body.
description.lore: Roxodon are remembered throughout history as steadfast defenders and unstoppable warriors capable of holding the line against impossible odds. Ancient stories tell of Roxodon champions standing alone at breached gates, charging through enemy formations, or shielding entire communities from destruction through sheer resilience and determination. Their horns became symbols of protection and perseverance across many cultures, representing the refusal to yield when others would break. Though feared in battle for their devastating charges and near-impenetrable defenses, Roxodon are equally respected for their calm wisdom and protective nature outside of war. Across countless regions, they are regarded as living bastions whose strength is measured not only by what they can destroy, but by what they are willing to protect.

species.feature_effect: Gain an additional +10 armor that can be healed or repaired If you move at least 20 ft toward a target before attacking, you have advantage on your next melee Attack.

species.name: Kangarans

description.physical: Kangarans are tall, athletic humanoids with powerful legs, long tails, and lean muscular builds adapted for speed, balance, and explosive movement. Their fur commonly appears in sandy browns, reddish earth tones, gray, or pale desert colors suited for blending into open plains and arid terrain. Massive leg muscles allow them to leap incredible distances and deliver devastating kicks capable of breaking bone or sending enemies sprawling. Their thick tails provide stability during movement and combat, helping them maintain balance even during rapid directional shifts. Kangarans move with restless energy and confidence, often appearing ready to spring into motion at a moment’s notice. Clothing among Kangarans favors lightweight travel gear, layered wraps, reinforced boots, and practical armor designed not to hinder mobility.
description.environment: Kangarans thrive across open plains, dry grasslands, deserts, and harsh frontier territories where endurance and mobility are essential for survival. Their communities are highly nomadic, traveling in small clans or roaming groups that follow seasonal weather patterns, migrating wildlife, and shifting resources. Camps are built quickly and efficiently using portable structures, hide shelters, and temporary fortifications designed for rapid movement. Kangarans are deeply familiar with dangerous terrain and hidden survival routes, often navigating environments outsiders consider barren or inhospitable.
description.culture: Kangaran culture values independence, resilience, martial skill, and loyalty to one’s kin. Every Kangaran is expected to contribute to the survival and protection of the group, whether through scouting, hunting, combat, or resource gathering. While naturally self-reliant and stubborn, Kangarans believe true strength comes from standing together when hardship threatens the clan. Ritualized combat holds great importance within their society, serving as a method of resolving disputes, proving worth, and determining leadership without unnecessary bloodshed. Strength is respected, but so are endurance, discipline, and the ability to survive difficult conditions without complaint. Their gatherings are often loud and competitive, filled with stories of travel, hunts, and legendary warriors.
description.lore: Kangarans are remembered throughout history as fierce nomadic warriors and relentless survivors capable of crossing impossible distances through harsh wilderness. Ancient stories describe Kangaran champions leaping across canyon gaps, defending entire caravans from raiders, or defeating far larger enemies through speed and overwhelming force. Their warrior traditions became legendary across frontier regions, where ritual combat and clan rivalries shaped generations of hardened fighters. Though outsiders sometimes mistake them for aggressive wanderers, Kangarans possess strong communal bonds and unwavering loyalty toward those they consider family. Across deserts and plains alike, they are respected as proud travelers who refuse to bow to hardship or surrender their freedom.

species.feature_effect: You can jump half your movement as part of a melee Attack, dealing an additional [[1d4]] Impact damage and knocking the target Prone if they fail a Strength check.

species.name: Wombarn

description.physical: Wombarn are stout, heavily built humanoids with dense muscle, thick fur, and powerful claws designed for digging and excavation. Their broad frames and low center of gravity make them exceptionally stable and difficult to move once planted. Fur coloration commonly ranges through earthy browns, dark grays, sandy tans, and muted reds suited for underground and rugged environments. Wombarn possess thick limbs, reinforced shoulders, and durable hides that allow them to work tirelessly through stone, soil, and debris. Though not fast-moving, their strength and endurance are remarkable, especially in confined terrain. Their clothing favors durable workwear, reinforced belts, heavy gloves, and practical gear built for labor, tunneling, and defense.
description.environment: Wombarn communities are most commonly found in hillsides, rocky badlands, forests, and underground tunnel systems where they construct extensive burrow settlements beneath the surface. Their homes are engineering marvels built to withstand cave-ins, floods, predators, and siege alike. Reinforced tunnels, hidden escape routes, storage chambers, and layered defensive passages are common features of Wombarn architecture. Even their smallest settlements are carefully planned for long-term stability and survival, often expanding over generations into sprawling underground strongholds.
description.culture: Wombarn culture values hard work, practicality, resilience, and creating things meant to endure. Builders, miners, engineers, defenders, and laborers hold highly respected positions within society, and every member of the community is expected to contribute in meaningful ways. Wombarn are famously stubborn and persistent, viewing perseverance as one of the highest virtues a person can possess. They prefer straightforward solutions and honest labor over unnecessary complexity or showmanship. Though often quiet and reserved around outsiders, Wombarn communities are deeply loyal and dependable once trust is established. Shared meals, communal construction projects, and the maintenance of ancestral tunnels are central parts of their social life.
description.lore: Wombarn are renowned throughout the world as master excavators and defensive builders capable of turning even hostile terrain into thriving and nearly impenetrable settlements. Ancient stories tell of Wombarn strongholds surviving earthquakes, invasions, and centuries of environmental collapse through sheer engineering brilliance and stubborn determination. Their tunnel systems are often filled with hidden chambers, defensive traps, and carefully preserved histories carved directly into stone walls. Though rarely conquerors, Wombarn are feared when forced into conflict, as once they decide to defend something, they become almost impossible to dislodge. Across countless regions, they are respected as symbols of endurance, reliability, and the quiet strength of those who build foundations that outlast generations.

species.feature_effect: You have a burrowing speed of half your movement, allowing you to move through loose soil or soft dirt. Additionally, you can use an action to create difficult terrain in a reach-range area by digging.

species.name: Crokagator

description.physical: Crokagators are massive reptilian humanoids with thick muscular bodies, heavy tails, and rough armored scales adapted for life in swamps and stagnant waters. Their coloration commonly ranges through murky greens, dark browns, black, and algae-stained grays that allow them to blend seamlessly into marshes and riverbanks. Broad jaws lined with crushing teeth give them terrifying bite strength, while powerful limbs allow sudden bursts of speed both in water and on land. Their eyes sit high along the head, often remaining visible above the waterline while the rest of the body stays hidden beneath the surface. Crokagators move with slow deliberate confidence until violence erupts, at which point they become explosively aggressive predators. Their armor and clothing tend to be minimal but practical, often crafted from bone, hide, swamp reeds, and trophies taken from dangerous hunts.
description.environment: Crokagators dominate swamps, marshlands, stagnant lakes, mangrove forests, and flooded river systems where they reign as apex predators and territorial guardians. Their settlements are commonly built atop raised mud platforms, hidden islands, partially submerged ruins, or fortified swamp villages connected by narrow waterways and concealed paths. These environments are intentionally difficult for outsiders to navigate, filled with deep water, unstable ground, thick fog, and hidden ambush points known intimately by local tribes. Waterways serve as lifelines within Crokagator society, functioning as roads, hunting grounds, and natural defensive barriers.
description.culture: Crokagator culture revolves around strength, territorial loyalty, survival, and tribal unity. Their tribes are tightly bonded communities where every member contributes to defense, hunting, and preservation of the territory. Strength is highly respected, but so are patience, cunning, and the ability to survive harsh environments. Warriors, hunters, shamans, and tribal protectors hold honored positions within society. Many disputes are settled through ritual combat, hunting trials, or displays of endurance rather than prolonged warfare. Though outsiders often see them as savage or brutal, Crokagators possess deep communal loyalty and fiercely defend their kin and ancestral waters from any threat.
description.lore: Crokagators are feared across countless regions as unstoppable swamp warriors and masters of ambush warfare. Ancient stories tell of entire armies vanishing within cursed marshlands after underestimating the tribes lurking beneath the water. Their thunderous bellows are infamous for spreading fear and panic before battle even begins, echoing across swamps like warnings from ancient predators. Among their most feared combat techniques is the devastating deathroll, where a pinned enemy is dragged into the water and torn apart through overwhelming rotational force. Though terrifying in battle, Crokagators are also respected as fiercely loyal allies who never abandon territory or companions once bonds are formed. In many frontier regions, their presence alone is enough to keep invaders and monsters away from the waters they claim as their own.

species.feature_effect: As an action, make a deep bellow. Enemies within reach must make a Mental check. On a failure, they take the Fear condition.

species.name: Jinra

description.physical: The Jinra are towering serpentfolk with immense muscular lower bodies and broad humanoid torsos that project both elegance and intimidation. Their scales commonly appear in deep emerald, obsidian, bronze, or jungle-black tones contrasted by gleaming golden bellies that shimmer like polished metal in torchlight. Many possess elaborate hood frills, horn-like crests, or ornamental facial scales that enhance their regal appearance. Jinra move with deliberate grace, their massive coils rippling with controlled strength capable of crushing stone or restraining prey effortlessly. Their eyes are sharp and unblinking, often carrying an unnerving calm that reflects their disciplined nature. Clothing among the Jinra favors layered silks, lacquered armor, ceremonial jewelry, and gold-adorned garments designed to emphasize status, lineage, and authority.
description.environment: Jinra civilization rises from dense jungle marshes, humid river kingdoms, and ancient flooded ruins reclaimed through disciplined engineering and careful control of the land. Their cities are built in towering tiers of dark stone, polished obsidian, lacquered wood, and gold-lined architecture surrounded by waterways, canals, and sacred pools. Massive temple-palaces dominate their settlements, filled with winding halls, hidden chambers, and monumental carvings depicting the Endless Coil. Trade districts and ceremonial arenas sit beside sacred sanctuaries where warriors, merchants, and philosophers gather beneath towering serpent iconography. Their territories are heavily guarded and designed to favor those familiar with the maze-like waterways and oppressive jungle terrain.
description.culture: Jinra culture revolves around discipline, control, beauty, and the pursuit of power through mastery rather than chaos. Wealth and status are deeply respected, but they are expected to reflect refinement, self-control, and purpose rather than simple greed. Trade is treated almost as a sacred ritual, with negotiation and commerce carrying the same weight as combat or governance. Their central philosophy, the Endless Coil, teaches that all power eventually returns to its source, shaping their beliefs around cycles of dominance, protection, and legacy. Jinra society values patience, strategic thinking, and emotional restraint, viewing uncontrolled anger or reckless ambition as signs of weakness. Warriors, merchants, judges, and ritualists all occupy respected roles within their rigid social order.
description.lore: The Jinra are remembered across the realms as ancient rulers, master negotiators, and terrifying conquerors whose influence spread through both trade and fear. Legends claim their earliest dynasties emerged from sacred marshes where colossal serpent spirits taught them the principles of the Endless Coil. Throughout history, Jinra empires became known for their immense wealth, disciplined armies, and ability to manipulate rivals through diplomacy as easily as force. Their coils became symbols of both protection and domination, representing the belief that true power comes from controlling what others cannot. Though many outsiders fear the Jinra for their calculating nature and overwhelming presence, few can deny the beauty, sophistication, and terrifying efficiency of the kingdoms they have built from swamp and stone.

species.feature_effect: You have advantage on Grappling checks. When you maintain a grapple, you may deal [[1d4]] Impact damage as an off-action.

species.name: Rua

description.physical: Rua are large humanoid turtles known for their durable shells, calm demeanor, and powerful resilient bodies. Their thick shells vary widely in color and pattern, ranging through mossy greens, weathered browns, dark stone tones, and oceanic blues marked by scars, carvings, or painted symbols tied to personal history and lineage. Broad limbs and dense musculature give Rua immense physical strength and endurance, though their movements are usually steady and deliberate rather than fast. Their shells serve not only as natural armor, but also as deeply personal extensions of identity, often modified with compartments, tools, charms, or supplies carried during long journeys. Many Rua can use their shells as a form of living storage, commonly referred to as a “Shell of Holding,” allowing them to transport treasured belongings and survival gear wherever they roam. Clothing among Rua tends to be simple and practical, favoring layered robes, travel cloaks, and straps designed to work comfortably around the shell.
description.environment: Rua are nomadic by nature, traveling between coastlines, rivers, wetlands, ancient roads, and remote settlements across the world. While some establish small villages near lakes or sheltered bays, many prefer a wandering lifestyle guided by pilgrimage routes, seasonal travel, or personal vows of protection. Their camps are quiet and communal, often built around shared fires, storytelling circles, and temporary shrines honoring wisdom, memory, and guardianship. Rua are highly adaptable and capable of enduring difficult environments for long periods of time thanks to their resilience and careful preparation.
description.culture: Rua culture values wisdom, patience, courage, and the responsibility to protect others. They believe strength exists to shelter those who cannot defend themselves, and many spend their lives acting as guardians, mediators, wandering protectors, or spiritual guides. Storytelling and oral tradition are central to preserving Rua history, with great heroes and moral lessons carried across generations through travel and teaching. Their nomadic nature encourages humility and openness, leading many Rua to form strong bonds with people far outside their own communities. Though peaceful by disposition, Rua are fiercely loyal and will endure tremendous hardship to defend allies, sacred places, or innocent lives. Rest, reflection, and careful thought are considered virtues equal to martial strength within their philosophy.
description.lore: Rua are remembered throughout history as wandering guardians and symbols of enduring wisdom. The most famous among them is the legendary Hanoi, who is said to have ended a devastating war by taking a tyrant’s enchanted blade and carrying it beyond the reach of mortal ambition. Tales like these shaped the Rua reputation as gentle but immovable protectors willing to stand against overwhelming power when necessary. Their shells are viewed as sacred symbols of both burden and protection, representing the idea that wisdom is carried through life rather than worn as decoration. Across countless cultures, the arrival of a Rua traveler is often seen as a sign of safety, guidance, or the quiet presence of ancient strength walking patiently through the world.

species.feature_effect: Your shell gives you +10 armor. Hold double the amount of bulk in your shell.

species.name: Savra

description.physical: Savra are lean, muscular reptilian humanoids distinguished by their elegant posture, refined features, and vividly patterned scales. Their coloration varies greatly between bloodlines, appearing in deep emeralds, desert golds, obsidian blacks, crimson reds, pale ivory, and countless intricate combinations marked by stripes, crests, or natural geometric patterns. Many possess sharp facial features, narrow eyes, decorative horns or frills, and long tails that enhance their poised and controlled appearance. Savra move with measured grace and confidence, rarely appearing hurried or uncertain. Their scales are naturally resilient, and their bodies possess exceptional recovery abilities that allow them to heal and overcome physical strain far faster than most species. Clothing among the Savra favors elegant layered garments, jewelry, ceremonial armor, and finely crafted attire that reflects both status and personal pride.
description.environment: Savra are found across ancient cities, jungle kingdoms, desert empires, and thriving trade centers where their long-standing cultures continue to influence surrounding regions. Their settlements are often architecturally grand, blending beauty, symmetry, and defensive practicality into sprawling palaces, elevated districts, and carefully planned streets. Even smaller Savra communities tend to preserve an atmosphere of refinement and tradition, maintaining libraries, courts, gardens, and monuments dedicated to ancestral history. Many Savra territories are built atop ruins or locations tied to their ancient lineage, reinforcing their deep connection to the past.
description.culture: Savra culture revolves around legacy, discipline, status, and the belief that their people descend from an ancient and elevated civilization. Regardless of personal wealth or rank, most Savra carry themselves with dignity and an ingrained sense of nobility. Etiquette, education, and presentation are highly valued within their society, with public composure treated as a reflection of personal strength. Savra communities emphasize resilience and self-mastery, encouraging individuals to recover quickly from hardship rather than dwell upon weakness or failure. Scholars, diplomats, warriors, artisans, and strategists all hold respected positions within society, provided they uphold the dignity and reputation of their lineage.
description.lore: Savra legends claim their people once ruled vast empires during an age when reptilian dynasties shaped civilization itself.
