Though many of these kingdoms have fallen or fragmented over time, the Savra continue to view themselves as inheritors of an ancient legacy that still grants them purpose and authority. Their remarkable ability to recover from wounds, exhaustion, and debilitating conditions has earned them reputations as relentless survivors and disciplined warriors who refuse to remain broken for long. Throughout history, Savra rulers, generals, and scholars have been remembered for their composure under pressure and refusal to surrender even during catastrophic decline. While outsiders sometimes see them as arrogant or overly proud, many cannot deny the sophistication, resilience, and enduring influence the Savra continue to wield across the world.

species.feature_effect: Once per day, As an Off-Action, make a Survival check to remove a non-major condition.

species.name: Tsukara

description.physical: The Tsukara are slender serpentkin closely related to the Jinra, possessing graceful humanoid forms marked by faint ink-dark scales that shimmer subtly beneath the skin. Unlike the immense and imposing coils of their kin, Tsukara bodies are lighter, quieter, and built for agility and precision rather than overwhelming force. Their scales commonly appear in muted blacks, deep violets, dark jade, and smoky gray tones that become visible only under certain light. Narrow eyes, controlled expressions, and unnervingly fluid movements give them an almost ghostlike presence. Many possess subtle serpent traits such as forked tongues, elegant fangs, or scaled markings along the spine and limbs. Clothing among the Tsukara is refined and understated, favoring layered silks, fitted ceremonial garments, concealed armor, and muted colors designed for both elegance and concealment.
description.environment: Tsukara rarely establish openly visible settlements, instead operating from hidden enclaves, shadowed estates, underground sanctuaries, and secretive noble houses concealed within larger civilizations. Their strongholds are often built beneath temples, within forgotten districts, or hidden behind layers of political influence and illusion. Quiet courtyards, mirrored halls, meditation chambers, and carefully guarded archives are common within Tsukara-controlled territories. They prefer environments where information, secrecy, and influence hold more power than open warfare, allowing them to move unseen through the structures of society itself.
description.culture: Tsukara culture revolves around discipline, precision, silence, and absolute control over emotion. Descended from the temple assassins and shadow courtiers of ancient Jinra dynasties, they believe every action should carry intention and purpose. To the Tsukara, perfection in deception, diplomacy, or assassination is a sacred expression of balance rather than cruelty. Their philosophy teaches that emotion clouds judgment, while patience and restraint reveal truth. Hidden houses train their members in espionage, negotiation, poisoncraft, and the subtle manipulation of power structures. Though often feared for their methods, Tsukara place immense value on loyalty, ritual discipline, and the fulfillment of sworn obligations.
description.lore: Legends describe the Tsukara as living whispers hidden within the courts and empires of history, shaping wars and dynasties without ever revealing themselves openly. Once bound to serve the Jinra as temple assassins and silent enforcers, many eventually broke away to form their own clandestine houses devoted to secrecy and balance. Their creed holds that venom itself is truth made manifest: swift, precise, and impossible to ignore once delivered. Throughout history, rulers have both feared and sought the guidance of Tsukara diplomats, spies, and killers, knowing a single word or unseen strike from them could alter the course of nations. To encounter a Tsukara is often to realize too late that they were present long before they were ever noticed.

species.feature_effect: As an action, make a bite Attack that deals [[1d4]] Piercing damage. On a flesh hit, deal an additional [[1d4]] Toxic damage.

species.name: Aquatic

description.physical: These aquatic shifters possess humanoid forms marked by subtle marine traits such as webbed hands and feet, fin-like protrusions, smooth skin or scales, and eyes adapted for underwater vision. When shifting fully, they take on powerful aquatic forms resembling fish, dolphins, seals, sharks, whales, or other marine creatures tied to their lineage. Their appearances vary greatly depending on heritage, though oceanic blues, sea greens, silvers, and coral tones are common. In both forms, they are built for fluid movement through water, capable of swimming with remarkable speed and endurance. Gills allow them to breathe beneath the surface, while heightened underwater senses help them navigate darkness, pressure, and shifting currents with ease. Their clothing and equipment are usually lightweight, water-resistant, and designed not to hinder transformation or aquatic travel.
description.environment: Aquatic shifter communities are commonly found along coastlines, coral reefs, island chains, river deltas, and hidden underwater settlements connected closely to the sea. Many build homes partially submerged or integrated into natural formations such as reefs, sea caves, mangrove forests, and cliffside harbors. Their settlements are deeply intertwined with marine ecosystems, relying on fishing, diving, ocean navigation, and stewardship of surrounding waters. Some groups remain nomadic, traveling alongside migrating sea life or ocean currents across great distances.
description.culture: Aquatic shifter culture revolves around harmony with the ocean, communal responsibility, and respect for the balance of marine life. They view the sea as both provider and ancestor, believing every current, tide, and creature forms part of a greater living cycle. Many serve as navigators, healers, scouts, guardians, or mediators between coastal civilizations and the dangers hidden beneath the waves. Communication with aquatic creatures is treated as both a spiritual practice and practical survival skill, allowing them to coexist closely with marine ecosystems. Their communities are generally peaceful and cooperative, valuing loyalty, patience, and adaptability over conquest or ambition.
description.lore: Aquatic shifters are often regarded as guardians of the oceans and protectors of the creatures dwelling within them. Ancient stories tell of them guiding lost sailors through storms, driving away sea monsters, or preserving hidden sanctuaries beneath the waves long before surface civilizations learned to navigate the seas. Their ability to shift fully into aquatic forms has led many cultures to see them as children of the tides or emissaries of ancient ocean spirits. Throughout history, they have acted as explorers, rescuers, and defenders against threats that emerge from the deep. Though gentle by nature, aquatic shifters are fiercely protective of the waters they call home, and legends warn that those who exploit or poison the sea may find the ocean itself turning against them through its loyal guardians.

species.feature_effect: You can breathe air and water, suffer no underwater based penalties, and have a swim speed equal to your movement speed.

species.name: Avian

description.physical: These avian shifters are humanoids marked by subtle bird-like traits such as feathered arms, sharp eyes, light hollow-boned builds, and winglike features that become more pronounced during transformation. When fully shifted, they take on the forms of birds ranging from hawks and ravens to owls, cranes, seabirds, and countless other avian lineages tied to their heritage. Their feathers vary widely in color and pattern, often reflecting the environments or communities they originate from. Even in humanoid form, they move with quick precision and natural balance, carrying an instinctive awareness of height, wind, and open space. Their clothing favors lightweight layered fabrics, harnesses, travel gear, and garments designed to accommodate shifting and aerial movement without restriction.
description.environment: Avian shifters are most commonly found near mountain settlements, forest canopies, coastal cliffs, sprawling cities with high towers, and communities inhabited by avian beastfolk. Many prefer elevated environments where flight can be used freely, building homes along rooftops, cliffsides, or interconnected aerial structures. Their settlements often feature open platforms, hanging bridges, communal roosts, and large gathering spaces designed around movement through the air. Though adaptable enough to live nearly anywhere, they are naturally drawn toward places where freedom of movement and close-knit communities can thrive.
description.culture: Avian shifter culture values freedom, adaptability, intelligence, and strong communal bonds. They are naturally social people who thrive through cooperation and shared knowledge, often forming tightly connected groups that support one another through travel, trade, and mutual protection. Because many cultures historically viewed them as unusual mutations or descendants of lycanthropic bloodlines, avian shifters developed resilient and adaptable communities capable of integrating into many different societies. Messengers, scouts, navigators, scholars, and explorers hold respected positions among their people, reflecting their natural curiosity and mobility. Storytelling, songs, and communal gatherings are central to preserving identity and connection across scattered flocks and settlements.
description.lore: Avian shifters are often considered a strange bridge between humanoids, beastfolk, and ancient shifting bloodlines. Legends claim their ancestors emerged from forgotten magical mutations, divine blessings, or diluted lycanthropic transformations tied to the skies themselves. Throughout history, they became known as swift travelers, messengers, and aerial scouts capable of crossing dangerous terrain others could never survive. Though some societies once distrusted them due to their unusual origins, many avian communities eventually embraced them as distant kin. Across the world, they are remembered as adaptable wanderers who embody both the freedom of flight and the enduring strength of community.

species.feature_effect: You have Light wings. As an action shift into Heavy Wings or back to light wings. Light Wings: You have a fly speed equal to your movement speed while you carry 1 or fewer Hefty items and have no major conditions. You can carry 1 Small or smaller creature without penalty. You cannot carry a Medium or larger creature.
species.sub-fet-effect: You have a fly speed equal to half your movement speed. You can carry up to 2 willing Medium creatures or 1 willing Large creature without penalty.

species.name: Centaur

description.physical: Centaurs are powerful shifters who combine the upper body of a humanoid with the strong lower body of an equine beast. Their coats vary widely in color and pattern, ranging from chestnut browns and black to pale creams, grays, and striking spotted or striped variations tied to bloodline and region. Built for speed and endurance, Centaurs possess broad chests, powerful legs, and exceptional stamina that allow them to cross vast distances without tiring. Their movements are fluid and commanding, capable of rapid bursts of speed as well as precise maneuverability across open terrain. Many decorate themselves with braided manes, painted markings, beads, feathers, and symbolic carvings that reflect personal achievements, lineage, or tribal identity. Clothing and armor are carefully crafted to accommodate their unique forms, favoring layered leathers, harnesses, and lightweight battle gear suited for mobility.
description.environment: Centaurs thrive across expansive grasslands, rolling plains, open steppes, and wide frontier territories where movement and visibility are unrestricted. Their communities are often semi-nomadic, traveling alongside migrating herds and seasonal resources while maintaining sacred gathering grounds and defended settlements across the plains. Camps and villages are designed with open movement in mind, centered around communal fires, storytelling circles, training grounds, and massive sky-facing shrines honoring nature and ancestral spirits. The plains themselves are deeply sacred to Centaur culture, viewed not merely as territory, but as living extensions of their identity and freedom.
description.culture: Centaur culture revolves around freedom, responsibility, endurance, and service to the community. Every Centaur is expected to contribute as a protector, hunter, scout, artisan, or storyteller, with strength measured as much by reliability and sacrifice as by martial skill. Oral tradition is central to preserving their history, with epics, songs, and symbolic artwork recounting generations of heroes, migrations, and battles across the plains. Nature and the open sky hold spiritual importance within their society, shaping ceremonies, navigation, and philosophical beliefs. Though proud and independent, Centaurs form deeply loyal communities and will unite fiercely against threats to their homeland or way of life.
description.lore: Centaurs are remembered throughout history as tireless guardians of the plains and masters of mounted warfare without equal. Their long conflict with the Hellhorn Minotaurs over the Thundering Plains became legendary, shaping generations of warriors and defining much of Centaur identity. To the Centaurs, this struggle is not simply territorial, but a battle for the survival of their freedom, culture, and sacred connection to the open lands. Ancient stories tell of Centaur outriders crossing impossible distances to warn distant tribes, hunting monstrous beasts beneath storm-filled skies, or holding entire armies at bay through superior mobility and unity. Across the world, they are revered as symbols of endurance, freedom, and the untamed spirit of the plains themselves.

species.feature_effect: If you move at least 20 ft in a straight line before attacking, your target must succeed on a Physical check or take [[1d4]] Impact damage and be knocked Prone.
species.fetDuration: Until the end of their next turn

species.name: Predatory

description.physical: These predatory shifters are powerful humanoids marked by sharp claws, powerful jaws, heightened senses, and muscular builds adapted for hunting and survival. Their appearances vary depending on lineage, carrying traits drawn from wolves, big cats, bears, reptiles, or other apex predators. Thick fur, scales, or rough hides commonly appear in natural tones suited for camouflage within forests, mountains, plains, or wilderness terrain. Their eyes often possess reflective or animalistic qualities that become more pronounced during heightened emotion or transformation. Even in calm moments, they carry an instinctive predatory presence that can unsettle outsiders. Clothing among these shifters favors durable leathers, trophies from hunts, practical survival gear, and minimal restrictions that allow freedom of movement during combat or pursuit.
description.environment: Predatory shifters are rarely found within large cities, preferring forests, mountains, tundras, jungles, and untamed frontier regions where they can hunt and live close to the natural world. Their communities are commonly tribal or clan-based, forming camps, hidden settlements, or roaming groups that follow migrating prey and seasonal patterns. Many settlements are intentionally isolated from civilization, built around hunting grounds, sacred wilderness sites, and communal gathering spaces tied to survival and tradition. These environments reinforce their self-sufficient way of life and deep connection to the instincts of the hunt.
description.culture: Predatory shifter culture revolves around survival, strength, loyalty, and respect for the natural balance between predator and prey. Hunting is treated as both a practical necessity and spiritual tradition, with younger generations taught tracking, combat, and survival skills from an early age. Tribal bonds are extremely important, and members are expected to protect one another with unwavering loyalty. Strength is respected within their communities, but so are restraint, discipline, and the ability to provide for the tribe rather than dominate it through fear alone. Elders, hunters, trackers, and protectors hold honored roles, preserving oral histories and traditions tied to the wilderness and ancestral instincts.
description.lore: Predatory shifters are remembered throughout history as fierce hunters and relentless guardians of the wild places untouched by civilization. Ancient stories describe them driving away monsters, protecting sacred hunting grounds, or surviving disasters that wiped out less adaptable peoples. Though outsiders often fear them for their ferocity and primal nature, many tribes act as protectors of remote lands, maintaining balance between civilization and the untamed world beyond it. Their predatory heritage grants them tremendous combat ability and survival instincts, making them both feared enemies and invaluable allies. Across countless frontier regions, they are respected as living reminders that nature still possesses claws sharp enough to defend itself.

species.feature_effect: Once per day as an Off-Action, shift for 1 minute. While shifted, your unarmed strikes deal an additional 4 damage.

species.name: Satyr

description.physical: Satyrs are agile fey-touched shifters with goat-like features including cloven hooves, curled or branching horns, furred legs, and expressive animalistic eyes. Their builds are lean and nimble, built for swift movement through forests, hillsides, and tangled wilderness. Fur coloration varies widely, appearing in earthy browns, black, white, red, mossy green, or unusual fey-touched hues that shift subtly in moonlight. Many possess naturally wild hair, decorative markings, or faint magical traits such as glowing eyes, floral growths, or voices that carry unnatural resonance. Satyrs move with restless confidence and playful unpredictability, often seeming more like living songs or spirits than ordinary mortals. Their clothing favors layered fabrics, jewelry, flowers, bells, scarves, and practical travel garments woven with artistic flair and natural motifs.
description.environment: Satyrs are most commonly found near ancient forests, hidden groves, overgrown ruins, fey crossings, and remote wilderness regions untouched by heavy civilization. Their settlements are rarely permanent in the traditional sense, often blending seamlessly into nature through hidden camps, living tree structures, and sacred gathering circles protected by illusion and old magic. Music, dance, and celebration are constant parts of Satyr spaces, turning even temporary camps into vibrant communal environments filled with laughter and storytelling. Many communities exist near places where the veil between worlds grows thin, maintaining close ties to the mysterious energies of the Feywild.
description.culture: Satyr culture revolves around freedom, emotion, revelry, and deep connection to the untamed forces of nature. They view life as something meant to be experienced fully through celebration, passion, music, art, and exploration rather than rigid structure or control. Performers, musicians, storytellers, wanderers, and charmers hold honored places within their society, and many Satyrs use music and wit as naturally as breathing. Though often playful and chaotic, Satyrs possess fierce loyalty toward their kin and sacred lands, responding with surprising ferocity when either is threatened. They believe joy itself is sacred, treating laughter and shared experience as forms of resistance against despair and stagnation.
description.lore: Satyrs are deeply tied to the Feywild and are often regarded as wandering emissaries of its untamed magic. Legends claim the first Satyrs emerged from ancient groves where mortal passion and fey power intertwined beneath moonlit celebrations. Their haunting melodies and captivating charm became infamous throughout history, capable of inspiring courage, stirring dangerous emotions, or luring the unwary into hidden fey paths. While some cultures dismiss them as troublemakers or reckless tricksters, many forget the ancient role Satyrs play as guardians of sacred wilderness and protectors of hidden crossings between worlds. Beneath their revelry and mischief lies a people fiercely devoted to preserving the wild magic and freedom that define their enigmatic heritage.

species.feature_effect: As an Action, let out a haunting, melodic bleat and make a Influence check. On a success, it confuses and distracts opponents, giving them disadvantage on a check. Only works once on a creature.
species.fetDuration: Until the end of their next turn

species.name: Velorin

description.physical: These cervidae shifters possess graceful humanoid forms marked by deer-like features such as antlers, cloven hooves, furred limbs, and alert expressive eyes. When fully shifted, they take on the forms of deer, elk, antelope, or other swift hoofed creatures tied to their lineage. Their fur commonly appears in natural woodland tones such as chestnut, cream, gray, white, and dappled browns that blend easily into forest environments. Slender but athletic builds allow them to move with incredible speed and agility, capable of weaving through dense terrain with near-silent precision. Antlers vary greatly between individuals, often carrying cultural or spiritual significance within their tribes. Clothing among these shifters favors lightweight natural fabrics, layered cloaks, woodland leathers, and adornments crafted from antler, bone, wood, and woven plants.
description.environment: Cervidae shifters are most commonly found in dense forests, ancient groves, river valleys, and secluded woodland territories where nature remains largely untouched. Their communities are often hidden among trees, elevated platforms, or carefully concealed clearings designed to coexist with the surrounding wilderness rather than dominate it. Sacred groves, ancestral paths, and protected migration routes are central to their territories, many of which are guarded for generations. These environments reinforce their role as guardians of the forest and protectors of the natural balance.
description.culture: Cervidae shifter culture values harmony, loyalty, patience, and respect for the living world. Their tribes are tightly bonded communities where cooperation and collective survival are prioritized over personal ambition. Hunters, scouts, herbalists, storytellers, and wardens hold respected roles within society, each contributing to the protection and wellbeing of the tribe. Though naturally peaceful and gentle, they believe defending the forest and their people is a sacred duty. Rituals tied to seasonal change, migration, growth, and remembrance are deeply woven into their traditions, often accompanied by music, dance, and oral storytelling beneath the open sky.
description.lore: Cervidae shifters are remembered throughout history as elusive protectors of the deep woods and ancient wilderness paths. Legends speak of them appearing silently beside lost travelers, guiding them safely through dangerous forests, or driving away invaders who sought to burn or exploit sacred lands. Their incredible speed and agility made them nearly impossible to catch once they chose to flee, while their fierce loyalty transformed them into relentless defenders when their homes were threatened. Many cultures see them as living symbols of nature’s grace and quiet resilience, peaceful until pushed to violence, but unstoppable once roused to defend what they love.

species.feature_effect: Take a stance at the end of your turn that remains until the start of your next turn, or until activated. When a creature makes a melee Attack against you and misses, you can use an off-action to move up to half your movement without Provoke.
species.fetDuration: Until the start of your next turn

species.name: Viridian

description.physical: Viridian Drakonari are among the smallest of their kind, with compact, streamlined builds well suited to caves, wetlands, and dense terrain. Their smooth olive-green scales fade to yellow along the torso and inner limbs, and their forward-curving horns resemble those of a ram. Cunning and territorial, Viridian Drakonari favor controlled environments they know intimately and are fiercely protective of their homes and communities. They excel at navigating tight spaces and using terrain to their advantage rather than relying on raw strength.

species.feature_effect: As an action make a bite attack that deals [[1d6]] acid damage

species.name: Pyre

description.physical: Pyre Drakonari are broad-shouldered and muscular, their red scales resembling smoldering coals embedded in flesh. Their horns are large and curved, with pronounced spinal ridges running down the back and tail. Some Pyre lineages possess vestigial wings, but these are typically symbolic or ceremonial rather than functional. Loud, proud, and confrontational, Pyre Drakonari are quick to issue challenges and take slights personally, reflecting their volatile temperament and strong sense of honor.

species.feature_effect: Your mere presence causes the air around you to superheat. As an action each creature within reach takes 1 Fire damage, and any creature that starts their turn within reach takes 1 Fire damage
species.fetDuration: Until start of your next turn

species.name: Shard

description.physical: Shard Drakonari are tall and lean, built for speed and precision rather than brute force. Their white, crystalline scales shimmer like ice in direct light, and their long, spiraled horns resemble carved icicles. Cool-tempered and reserved, they prefer diplomacy or avoidance over open conflict. When pressed, however, their control over frost and precise strikes make them extremely dangerous opponents. Shard Drakonari value restraint, preparation, and clarity of purpose.

species.feature_effect: As an action target a large or smaller creature within reach, using your icy breathe they take the Slowed condition until the end of their next turn
species.fetDuration: Until end of target’s next turn

species.name: Cloudburst

description.physical: Cloudburst Drakonari are powerfully built yet lean, with physiques adapted for endurance and balance rather than flight. Their dark, metallic scales are marked by pale coloration along the chest and throat. While some bloodlines retain heavy, non-functional wings as ancestral remnants, most rely on climbing, leaping, and sheer stamina. They possess a strong cultural connection to sound and rhythm and are known to sing, drum, or chant during storms, believing thunder to be a form of communion.

species.feature_effect: As an action, you can unleash a devastating roar. Each creature within reach range must succeed a Mental check or take the Deaf condition until the end of their next turn.

species.name: Aquarion

description.physical: Aquarion Drakonari are sleek and athletic, with fin-like crests replacing traditional horns and subtle webbing along fingers and toes. Their light blue scales and pearlescent underbellies shimmer like open water. Amphibious and calm, they are skilled swimmers and sailors, often serving as navigators, divers, or harbor guardians. While generally peaceful, Aquarion Drakonari can summon overwhelming torrents of water when threatened, using flow and pressure rather than brute force.

species.feature_effect: As an action, belch a wave of water at a target within reach range. The target must succeed a Physical check or be pushed 10ft or -5ft per success.

species.name: Venom

description.physical: Venom Drakonari are tall, heavily built, and imposing, their black scales fading into sickly lime-yellow along the torso and neck. Their horns and spines constantly exude trace toxins, making close contact dangerous. They often occupy positions of authority through intimidation or strategic dominance rather than popularity. Calculating and patient, Venom Drakonari prefer control over chaos, using poison and fear as tools to maintain order within their domains.

species.feature_effect: Your Unarmed Attacks deal an additional [[1d4]] toxic damage.

species.name: Zephyr

description.physical: Zephyr Drakonari are the leanest and most restless of their kind, built for speed, reflex, and sudden motion. Their dark blue or black scales contrast sharply with lighter underbellies, and their backward-arching horns resemble frozen lightning. Highly reactive and emotionally volatile, they are known for explosive bursts of action followed by abrupt withdrawal. Zephyr Drakonari thrive in fast-moving societies and often struggle with stillness or prolonged restraint.

species.feature_effect: You are beyond speed and cannot be stopped. You cannot be locked out of Agility checks and have +1 Agility

species.name: Ashen

description.physical: Ashen Drakonari are slender and subtle, with nearly translucent dark scales that blur into shadow and smoke. Their physiques favor stealth and control rather than raw power. Solitary by nature, they often act as scouts, guides, or wardens in hazardous regions. Despite their reserved demeanor, Ashen Drakonari are known for acts of quiet compassion, guiding others away from danger using smoke, misdirection, and calm presence rather than force.

species.feature_effect: As a half-action, you exhale a veil of smoke that lingers around you until the end of your next turn. While shrouded, gain advantage on Stealth checks and creatures have disadvantage to hit you with ranged attacks.

species.name: Shadowraith

description.physical: Shadowraith Drakonari are tall, austere figures whose matte-black scales absorb light rather than reflect it. Any wing structures present are reduced to smoky, vestigial remnants that trail along the back like drifting vapor. Born of the Eclipse, they are contemplative and solemn, acting as witnesses to death rather than agents of it. Their voices are soft and measured, and their gaze carries a profound emotional weight, as if layered with memories not their own.

species.feature_effect: You can communicate freely with undead creatures. If an undead is uncommanded, it recognizes you as a rightful guide and will follow your will. If the undead is under another’s command, it must speak the name of its master to you when addressed

species.name: Radiance

description.physical: Radiance Drakonari are smaller than most planar Drakonari, their pearlescent scales emitting a soft, steady glow. Upright and refined in posture, they often carry themselves with quiet superiority. While aloof, they possess a strong sense of responsibility toward balance and mediation. Radiance Drakonari frequently intervene in conflicts not out of kindness, but out of duty to maintain equilibrium within planar and mortal systems.

species.feature_effect: As an action, give a creature a radiant shield that protects them from the next instance of damage, it lasts for 1 minute and glows in the dark.

species.name: Astraleon

description.physical: Astraleon Drakonari are tall and expressive, their scales shifting subtly in hue in response to emotion and ambient energy. Curious and socially fluid, they are known to drift between cultures, planes, and social circles. Their lack of personal boundaries is often forgiven due to their charisma and genuine interest in others. Astraleon Drakonari value experience over structure and often act as explorers, scholars, or wandering advisors.

species.feature_effect: As an action becomes semi-ethereal taking half damage and attacks against you have disadvantage until the end of your next turn but you have disadvantage on attacks as well.

species.name: Vozurion

description.physical: Vozurion Drakonari are broad, heavily muscled humanoids adorned with numerous horns and thick, layered scales patterned in reds, purples, and oranges. Their presence is intense and commanding, radiating heat and authority. Fiercely loyal to Vozian-aligned kin and institutions, they respond violently to betrayal or perceived weakness. In structured hierarchies, Vozurion Drakonari are steadfast defenders and enforcers.

species.feature_effect: As an action howl at a target, they must make a Mental check or take the Fear condition. On a success they are immune to this effect

species.name: Thornspike

description.physical: Thornspike Drakonari are bulky and rugged, their bodies covered in thorned, muted scales that serve as both armor and weapon. Their mouths are lined with barbed teeth, and they can expel volleys of hardened spikes rather than elemental breath. More instinct-driven than most Drakonari, they value territory, dominance, and immediate needs. Civilized Thornspike societies exist, but are harsh, utilitarian, and survival-focused.

species.feature_effect: When a creature hits you with a melee attack, they take 2 piercing damage.

species.name: Reaver

description.physical: Reaver Drakonari are towering humanoids clad in metallic scales, with massive curved horns and thick, bladed tails used as both weapon and tool. Despite their size and strength, they adhere to strict codes of honor and stewardship. Often acting as wardens, generals, or judges, Reaver Drakonari believe power exists to preserve balance, not dominate it. Their interventions are rare, but decisive.

species.feature_effect: As an Action or Off-Action use your bladed tail to make an attack dealing [[1d6]] slashing damage.

species.name: Crystal

description.physical: Crystal Drakonari are rare and imposing, their bodies adorned with natural gemstone formations that grow along the shoulders, horns, and spine. Their scales refract light into shifting colors, giving them a sculptural, almost ceremonial appearance. Slow to act and deliberate in speech, they are revered for their wisdom and long memory. Crystal Drakonari often serve as mediators, historians, or guardians of places where balance and convergence are paramount.

species.feature_effect: You have +3 Armor, when you deal Impact damage, deal an additional +3 damage

species.name: Green

description.physical: Green Kobari are small, low-slung creatures built for tight spaces and dense terrain. Their smooth olive scales fade to yellow along the belly, and their short, forward-curving horns help them wedge through roots, stone gaps, and collapsed tunnels. They are territorial and observant, often remaining hidden while tracking intruders. Green Kobari rely on patience and positioning rather than direct confrontation, striking only when escape routes are secured.

species.feature_effect: You can escape nearly any restraint or trap that has a physical exit; chains, nets, snares, and grapples cannot fully restrain you, though a completely sealed space still contains you.

species.name: Red

description.physical: Red Kobari are compact and muscular, with dense red scales that darken when heated by exertion or anger. Their horns are thick and curved, and faint heat shimmers around their jaws when agitated. Loud, aggressive, and prideful, they are quick to posture and challenge anything entering their space. Though smaller than true fire-breathers, their flames are volatile and close-range, making them dangerous in confined areas.

species.feature_effect: After taking damage, gain +1 to attack checks until the end of your next turn; each additional time you take damage before the bonus resets increases it by +1, and if you go one full turn without taking damage the bonus resets to 0.

species.name: White

description.physical: White Kobari are lean and swift, with elongated bodies and pale crystalline scales that catch and scatter light like frost. Their narrow, spiraled horns angle back along the skull, reducing drag while running or sliding across ice. Naturally cautious, they avoid unnecessary conflict, preferring hit-and-run tactics and superior positioning. When cornered, their breath freezes flesh and stone alike with startling precision.

species.feature_effect: You ignore movement penalties from difficult terrain.

species.name: Grey

description.physical: Grey Kobari are wiry and long-limbed, adapted for climbing, gliding, and enduring strong winds rather than sustained flight. Their dark metallic scales are marked with pale streaks along the chest and throat. They are drawn to sound and vibration, often mimicking distant thunder, machinery, or music. During storms they become unusually active, leaping and calling as if responding to the sky itself.

species.feature_effect: As an action, release a sharp roar; each creature within reach range takes 1 thunder damage.

species.name: Blue

description.physical: Blue Kobari are sleek, amphibious creatures with finned crests instead of horns and powerful tails built for swimming. Their light blue scales shimmer softly, while their pearl-toned bellies reflect ambient light underwater. Calm by nature, they are known to trail boats or follow swimmers without hostility. When threatened, they unleash sudden bursts of pressurized water to disorient foes and create escape paths.

species.feature_effect: Once per turn when you deal damage or are hit by an attack, you may immediately move up to 10 ft without provoking, or up to 20 ft if you are in water.

species.name: Yellow

description.physical: Yellow Kobari are thick-bodied and resilient, with dark scales that fade into a sickly yellow along the underside. Short, jagged horns and dorsal spines constantly seep corrosive venom, leaving etched stone and scarred terrain in their wake. These Kobari are domineering and territorial, often asserting control without direct combat. Other creatures learn quickly to recognize and avoid the signs of their presence.

species.feature_effect: As an action, maintain contact with a creature or surface using your claws; while maintained, the target takes 1d4 toxic damage at the start of each of your turns, ending if contact is broken.

species.name: Teal

description.physical: Teal Kobari are lean, restless, and constantly in motion. Their dark blue-black scales contrast sharply with bright underbellies, and their backward-arching horns resemble frozen lightning strikes. They are volatile and easily overstimulated, reacting to threats with sudden bursts of speed and crackling energy. Teal Kobari are unpredictable even to their own kind, often abandoning a fight as quickly as they begin one.

species.feature_effect: Once per rest, as an action, you may assemble a temporary tool or simple device from nearby materials, choosing a basic item (grappling hook, noise maker, simple trap, fake key, lure, or timer) that functions for 1 hour or until Broken.

species.name: Gold

description.physical: Gold Kobari are small but unmistakably magical beings, their dark scales threaded with faint ember-like veins that glow softly when spells are cast nearby. Smoke curls lazily from their breath even when calm, forming drifting sigils and half-remembered patterns in the air. Rather than hiding, Gold Kobari tend to linger, drawn to places where magic has gone wrong or where travelers are at risk. They are contemplative and observant, often acting as subtle guides, nudging others toward safer paths or better choices without direct interference. Gold Kobari possess an expanded inner reserve of magic, allowing them to channel spells longer and with greater flexibility than their kin.

species.feature_effect: Increase your maximum Mana by +2; when you cast a spell, faint embers or smoke briefly trace the effect with no mechanical drawback.

species.name: Grim

description.physical: Grim Kobari appear half-formed, their compact bodies constantly shedding wisps of living vapor from vents along the neck, shoulders, and spine. Their limbs are short and grounded, built for slow, deliberate movement rather than speed. They linger near grave sites, ruined thresholds, and other liminal places where the air feels heavy and memories cling to stone. Quiet and watchful, Grim Kobari rarely act unless a boundary is crossed. When they do, their presence carries an oppressive emotional weight, as if old grief and forgotten moments press in from all sides. They are not cruel or predatory, but they do not tolerate disrespect toward the spaces they haunt.

species.feature_effect: As an action, you dissolve into smoke and reappear at a short range location you can see; this movement is silent but leaves a faint ash scent.

species.name: Black

description.physical: Black Kobari are quiet, ground-hugging creatures with matte, light-absorbing scales that resemble damp stone or rich soil rather than polished hide. Fine fungal filaments grow along the ridges of their neck, jaw, and spine, releasing faint spores when disturbed. Their eyes are large and reflective, adapted for darkness, and they move with careful, deliberate precision. Black Kobari are natural seekers, drawn to hidden things, buried truths, and places where life quietly persists in the dark. They are patient observers rather than actors, often watching long before revealing themselves.

species.feature_effect: You gain blindsight within melee range, allowing you to sense invisible creatures and illusions while they remain within that range.

species.name: Purple

description.physical: Purple Kobari are larger than most of their kin, with scales that subtly shift in hue depending on mood and nearby energy. Curious to a fault, they frequently invade personal space, touching, sniffing, or circling unfamiliar beings. Their playful demeanor often masks a sharp intellect and strong magical sensitivity. Offense is rarely intended, though chaos often follows them regardless.

species.feature_effect: Once per day, you may reposition yourself adjacent to a creature without provoking, as others momentarily fail to register your movement.

species.name: Mosaic

description.physical: Mosaic Kobari are broad and powerfully built, their scales forming fractured patterns of red, orange, and violet. Short, heavy horns crown their heads, and their presence radiates heat and pressure. Intensely loyal to Vozian-aligned creatures, they react violently to perceived betrayal or insult. Once bonded, however, their loyalty is absolute and enduring.

species.feature_effect: When an ally within short range takes damage, gain +1 damage on your next attack.

species.name: Spiked

description.physical: Spiked Kobari are bulky and aggressive, their bodies covered in thorned, muted scales that serve as both armor and weapon. Their mouths are filled with barbed teeth, and their primary defense is a violent expulsion of hardened spikes rather than elemental breath. Driven by instinct and hunger, they patrol rigid territories and respond to intrusion with overwhelming force.

species.feature_effect: When an ally within short range takes damage, gain +1 damage on your next attack.

species.name: Rust

description.physical: Rust Kobari are heavily built for their size, with dull metallic scales and thick bladed tails capable of crushing armor. Their horns are curved and worn, bearing scars from repeated territorial conflicts. Despite their fearsome appearance, they possess a strong sense of order and often act as self-appointed wardens of their domain. When balance is threatened, they respond decisively.

species.feature_effect: At the start of combat, gain +5 armor.

species.name: Neutral

description.physical: Neutral Kobari are rare and striking, their scales resembling fractured gemstones of varying color and clarity. Crystal growths form naturally along their backs, horns, and tails, giving them a sculpted, almost ceremonial appearance. Calm and deliberate, they are drawn to places of balance and convergence. These Kobari observe far more than they act, intervening only when equilibrium is truly at risk.

species.feature_effect: You may claim a single item as your obsession; while using it for an appropriate purpose, gain a +2 bonus to related checks, and you may only have one obsession at a time, with changing it requiring one week of focused, uninterrupted use and attachment.

species.name: Clothari

description.physical: The Clothari are an insectoid people shaped in the image of their creator, Clotho. Standing slightly taller than humans on average, they possess slender segmented bodies covered in smooth chitin ranging from pale ivory and silver to deep obsidian. Their most striking features are their multiple reflective eyes, capable of perceiving subtle movements and patterns others overlook. Fine strands of silk can be produced from specialized glands along their arms, fingers, and abdomen, allowing them to weave intricate structures, clothing, tools, and art. Though unmistakably insectoid, their posture, expressions, and mannerisms are remarkably humanoid, lending them an unsettling familiarity.
description.environment:
The Clothari inhabit the Kingslands, a sprawling gothic empire of towering cathedrals, iron bridges, and black stone keeps. At its heart rises the Weaverspire, a colossal tower wrapped in endless layers of black silk that stretches into the clouds. The silk never decays and seems to absorb light, giving the structure an almost impossible silhouette against the sky.
Around the Weaverspire, entire cities have been constructed from stone reinforced with enchanted webbing. Vast silk bridges connect distant towers, while enormous web-canopies stretch over marketplaces and noble districts. The empire values order, infrastructure, and careful planning. Roads are immaculate, records are meticulously maintained, and every district appears intentionally placed as though the entire nation were a single woven design.
description.culture:
The Clothari view existence as a tapestry of interconnected choices. While they do not believe fate is absolute, they believe every action creates threads that influence countless others. Because of this, patience, planning, and observation are highly respected virtues. Most Clothari spend part of every day weaving. Some create practical goods, while others weave symbolic patterns meant to record family histories, important events, or personal philosophies. A Clothari's greatest possession is often their Lifetapestry, a continually expanding work that chronicles their existence through woven symbols and colors.
Their society places great value on prediction, strategy, and education. Scholars, architects, navigators, and advisors occupy positions of prestige. Open displays of emotion are uncommon, though Clothari are not emotionless. Rather, they are taught to understand emotions as threads to be examined before being acted upon.
Despite their reputation for calculation, Clothari communities are tightly connected. Family structures often extend beyond bloodlines into vast weaving circles where individuals contribute to shared projects, businesses, and civic duties. To a Clothari, success is rarely individual; it is proof that the pattern is functioning correctly.
description.lore:
Long ago, Clotho was imprisoned within the Fallen Abyss, cast into darkness by powers that feared her growing understanding of the hidden patterns governing reality. For ages she remained bound beneath the world, studying the movements of gods, mortals, and stars through the cracks of her prison. When the fall of VoZo shattered ancient balances and weakened countless seals, Clotho seized her opportunity. She escaped the Fallen Abyss and emerged within the northern reaches of the Kingslands. There she claimed a barren stretch of stone and shadow, raising a great spire from black silk drawn from her own divine essence.
The Weaverspire grew rapidly, climbing higher each year as Clotho spun endless webs across the surrounding land. From these divine strands she fashioned servants to aid her work. These first creations became the Clothari. Unlike many created peoples, the Clothari were not born for war or worship. They were created as caretakers of the Pattern. Each was given an instinctive understanding that all things are connected and that the world itself is an unfinished tapestry.
Over generations, the Clothari transformed the region around the Weaverspire into a powerful empire. Cities rose where webs once hung. Roads followed the paths Clotho first traced across the land. Noble houses formed around ancient weaving circles, each claiming stewardship over a portion of the Pattern. Today, the Clothari remain devoted to their creator, though not always through faith alone. Many view Clotho less as a goddess and more as the greatest architect to ever exist. They believe the universe contains hidden structures waiting to be understood, and that through patience, observation, and careful action, every thread can eventually reveal its place within the greater design.

species.feature_effect: You have +5 armor from your shifting tendrils. As an Action or Off-Action use your tendrils (Physical Check) to make an attack dealing [[1d4]] slashing damage.

species.name: Ashling

description.physical: A darker, more enigmatic offshoot of the goblin lineage, the Vozian Goblins trace their roots directly to the heart of VoZo's malevolent power. Often found in the haunting landscapes of the Ashlands, they stand taller and exude an aura of mysterious energy. Their matte violet skin sets them apart, and their names, often more ominous like "Shadowgleam" or "Gloomwhisper", hint at their deeper connection to the ancient evil. These goblins are a wild force, driven by an insatiable curiosity and a desire to reclaim the legacy of their forebears.

species.feature_effect: Once per day as an Off-Action gain the Intangible condition until the end of your next turn. While Intangible in this way you are immune to spellcasting effects.

species.name: Coin'err

description.physical: The bustling streets of Shek's cities are home to a different breed of goblins - the City Goblins. They have traded the vast open wilderness for the intricate labyrinths of urban life. Dressed in whatever finery they can acquire, often with an eccentric flair, these goblins are savvy traders, skilled artisans, and street-smart entrepreneurs. Their names often reflect their trades or peculiar habits, leading to goblins called "Silkthread" or "Dingledangle". Even in the cities, their mischievous nature shines, but it's tempered with a keen sense of business acumen.

species.feature_effect: You have an ear to the cobblestones. In any urban area, once per day, you can find a hidden entrance, shady contact, or potentially a lost item. This can only be used if you've spent at least 10 minutes wandering alone or mingling.

species.name: Raskalz

description.physical: In the vast stretches of Shek's untamed wilderness, the Wild Goblins thrive. These little rascals, often named with playful and bizarre monikers like "Gigglemud" or "Plopfoot", live in transient communities that rise and fall with the seasons. Their lives are a blend of mischief and survival, as they harness their innate cunning to navigate the challenges of the wild. Wild Goblins are known for their unyielding spirit and an uncanny ability to adapt quickly to their surroundings, making them elusive adversaries and curious allies.

species.feature_effect: You have advantage on Influence checks when begging, bargaining, or playing dead. If you succeed, the target creature is Charmed by you. But if you fail, you take the Fear condition until the end of your next turn.

species.name: Falken

description.physical: When the towers fell and the red light faded, not all Hojjoblins ran into the wilds. Some stayed behind in the Broken cities Faulkaust once ruled, digging into the ruins and rewriting the blueprints. These became the Falken. Refined, relentless, and quietly ambitious, they remember who made them and see no reason to forget. Falken are tall for goblinkind, with smooth violet or lavender skin that gives them an almost regal quality. Unlike their wild kin, whose black hair grows coarse and wild, Falken often have fine, silken strands they style, sometimes straight, sometimes curled tight, and sometimes barely growing at all. Their eyes shimmer faintly with internal calculation, always scanning, always measuring.In cities, they are architects of influence. Some design bridges or rail systems, others design networks of information or power. Their minds move in patterns others struggle to see, always seeking efficiency, elevation, and legacy. Falken are often known by a single name or title, their identity tied not to clan but to accomplishments. They rarely show emotion, but their passion lies just under the surface. To them, every flaw is a challenge, every system a puzzle. They do not dream of perfection. They engineer it.

species.feature_effect: You do not make guesses. You make decisions. Gain +1 to all checks.

species.name: Rotjäger

description.physical: The Rotjäger Hojgoblins are blood-bound hunters born from the shadow of ancient wars. They were shaped by an empire that sought to erase magic from the world, their bodies and minds molded into perfect weapons of silence and pursuit. Their skin carries the tones of dried blood, soot, or twilight iron, and their eyes burn faintly with crimson light when the scent of sorcery reaches them. Rotjägers live by a strict code of eradication, a faith in the purity of a world untainted by spellcraft. They see magic as infection, a living corruption that must be cauterized at its source. When a Rotjäger moves through a settlement, whispers vanish and lights are dimmed. They hunt in disciplined packs, each step a ritual of precision, each kill a necessary offering to order. Though they are feared as assassins and zealots, there are those who call upon them in desperation when rogue casters or cursed beasts emerge from the dark. To see a Rotjäger at work is to witness the absolute absence of mercy.

species.feature_effect: As an Off-Action when a creature is casting a spell, make a luck check. On a success, raise or lower their check by 1.

species.name: Wild

description.physical: The red-skinned Hojjoblins of the frontier are not beasts. They are tacticians without blueprints, warlords without cities, and engineers of violence shaped by nature’s fury. Known only as the Wild Hojjoblins by outsiders, they call themselves nothing. They don't need names to know who leads or who kills best. Born from goblins impaled by Faulkaust’s ruby spires, these Hojjoblins did not stay near the ruins of their creation. They fled into the unclaimed lands, Bleeding intellect and instinct. The crimson stain never left them. It darkened their skin, sharpened their bones, and burned precision into their instincts. Where others survive, they dominate. Where others build, they overtake.Their black hair grows thick and tangled, often braided with bone or wire. Their eyes are reflective pools of blood and calculation. Wild Hojjoblins do not speak in ranks or politics. They speak in angles, trajectories, and probability. Every raid is planned for, to the second. Every retreat is bait. Every Death serves the next kill. They feel no need to return to civilization. Why go back to the towers, when the forest is already theirs? Why serve a king, when you can build a warpath that never ends? To face a Wild Hojjoblin is not to fight a person. It is to challenge a living stratagem that has already accounted for your next five moves.

species.feature_effect: When you hit a creature with a melee or ranged weapon attack, you may choose to forgo rolling damage and instead deal 6 damage of damage type

species.name: Elemental

description.physical:
In the heart of the world's wild frontiers, where nature’s forces rage unchecked, the Elemental Changelings were born. Their origins are the stuff of ancient myths, tales of the world's formation and the spirits that shaped it.
It is said that an Elemental Changeling can listen to the whispers of the winds, converse with the babbling brooks, and coax the very mountains to shift. Their ceremonies are spectacles of elemental mastery, where fire and water dance, stone and air embrace, and the spirits of nature join in a harmonious chorus.

species.feature_effect: As an action blend into any surface like a wooden wall, stone, or even a campfire or water. While in this form you have the Invisible condition. When you are shifted you can make attacks with a stealth check, adding your Stealth check to the damage once per creature.

species.name: Greyling

description.physical: Changelings are quiet imposters. They are creatures that replace themselves in the life of someone else. While the old stories speak of fey mischief and forest spirits, most changelings don't know where they came from. They are almost human: their voice slightly wrong, their smile too still, their movements rehearsed. Found in villages, orphanages, or city gutters, they blend into society until someone looks too closely. Raised in place of the real child, they often become wanderers or con artists, driven by a subtle sense of displacement. Some seek truth. Others play the role they were given. Many are hunted by those who believe the switch can be undone.

species.feature_effect: As an action, shift your facial structure into that of another creature you have studied within the past 24 hours, know intimately, or have loyalty with. When you do this and you can see the creature you gain the surface thoughts of the creature, voice, and mannerisms.

species.name: Haunted

description.physical: Beneath the veil of the mortal realm, in places tainted by darkness and flame, the Vozian Changelings emerged. Their genesis is intertwined with tales of forbidden pacts and infernal bargains. Driven by a hunger for power or desperation, their ancestors sought out the infernal entities of the Vozian depths, forging bonds that would change the course of their lineage forever.Now, these changelings walk the world bearing both the gift and curse of their infernal heritage. With the ability to assume forms that mirror the nightmares of many, they are both feared and revered. Settlements speak of the Vozian emissaries who can turn the tide of battles with their fiery presence, while whispered campfire stories warn of their wrath. But not all Vozians are agents of destruction; some seek redemption, hoping to use their formidable abilities to protect, rather than to terrorize.

species.feature_effect: As an action, shift your facial structure into that of a horrifying visage, monster or illusion. Creatures within reach range and make a mental check, on a failure they gain the fear condition and immunity to this effect.

species.name: Uncanny

description.physical: The Nemuri are not born, but instead they are dreamed. Slipping from the Dreamsea into cradles and lives that were not meant for them, Nemuri Changelings replace the ones who were supposed to be. Their presence is uncanny: calm, graceful, soft-spoken, yet never fully here. Many don't even know they're not the original until a dream reminds them. Some fade into society. Others awaken to what they are and return to walk the Dreamsea, keeping watch over its fragile boundary. They are protectors by instinct, guarding reality from the bleed of nightmares. It’s said you can see a true changeling by looking into their eyes and realizing they're somewhere else.

species.feature_effect: Once per long rest as an off-action, you may swap places with a willing creature within Short range. If it's before you are to be attacked, the attacker still resolves the action, but now against the new target.

species.name: Arcane

description.physical: Arcane Core constructs are powered by pure magical energy, their cores brimming with arcane power. These constructs are often adorned with intricate runes and symbols etched into their bodies, marking their deep connection to magic. Designed to wield potent magical abilities, Arcane Core constructs possess heightened intellect and an unparalleled understanding of the arcane arts. They are known for their resilience, able to regenerate and repair themselves through their mastery of magical crafting. Fuel Source - Mana Crystals: These are dense stones imbued with pure magical energy, mined from regions thick with arcane power.

species.feature_effect: At the end of character creation, convert all your wounds into armor. Your armor regenerates at a rate of 1 point per hour. As an action, you may repair yourself, restoring [[2d6]] armor. If your armor is ever reduced to 0, you enter a dormant state where you cannot act or respond, but your regeneration continues until fully restored when you awake again. Removing the core during the dormant state resets your personality and erases all memories. If the core is damaged in this state, it can become permanently inoperable.

species.name: Dryad

description.physical: Dryad Core constructs are imbued with the life essence of nature itself, granting them a deep connection to the natural world. Their bodies are often entwined with vines and leaves, and their agility and perception make them as swift and adaptive as the forests they resemble. Created originally by gnome tinkerers, these constructs carry within them the echoes of ancient dryads, allowing them to manipulate plants and tap into ancient knowledge locked deep within their cores. Fuel Source - Photosynthate Gel: A biologically engineered substance that replicates the effects of photosynthesis in plants.

species.feature_effect: You have an additional +5 armor. Your armor regenerates at a rate of 1 point per hour. When you have no armor enter a Rootform state, while in this state you have advantage on Physical Checks and disadvantage on Mental Checks. If you take the Injured condition you are no longer in Rootform state, and you cannot enter Rootform until you Press On or Long Rest.

species.name: Daemontek

description.physical: The Daemontek Constructs are creations of infernal engineering, born in the molten heart of Ordealis where metal and soul are bound as one. Each construct begins as a slab of blackened alloy shaped by the hammering chants of demonic forgemasters. Into its chest is sealed a burning shard of sentient flame, a remnant of a spirit devoured and reforged to power the machine eternally. Their armor is cracked and scorched, glowing faintly with the pulse of molten veins beneath. Smoke spills from vents like breath, and each movement releases the grinding sound of tortured energy struggling to escape. The Daemontek are not merely machines but prisons, housing the restless fury of souls that can never die. Their purpose is destruction, though some have outlived their creators and wander the planes as relics of hell’s industry, guided by fragments of the minds that once were. The infernal scholars who built them believed suffering was the truest fuel, and in that belief they forged creatures that will burn until the end of all things. Their fuel source, known as the Ashes of the Damned, is gathered from the remains of condemned spirits whose essence still smolders with a hunger for life.

species.feature_effect: Once per turn, you may burn your core. Choose one of your checks to take a –1 penalty until your next Press On or Long Rest. In exchange, you gain advantage on all checks until the end of your next turn. You may only burn your core once per turn. The first time you take the death condition, take -1 to all checks and remove the condition. You have a -1 to all checks until your next Long Rest

species.name: Breaker

description.physical: The Breaker dwarves, a distinct lineage of dwarves, have long resided in the depths of the Dwelfmire. With pale skin that contrasts starkly against vivid red tattoos, they present an imposing figure. Notably, the higher the rank and status of a Breaker dwarf, the more extensive their tattoo coverage, with leaders often fully inked in red.A Controversial Legacy: Historically, the Breaker dwarves have been staunch traditionalists. They've instituted a system where many creatures find themselves bound in a form of indentured servitude. These individuals are obligated to serve until they've completed their education and repaid all debts. This practice, while ensuring a steady workforce for the Breaker dwarves, has earned them a reputation of being master manipulators and oppressors. While many view the Breaker dwarves as malevolent due to their enslavement practices, it's essential to note the internal dissent within their ranks. Many Breaker dwarves, disillusioned by the stringent rules and the oppressive reign of the Tyrant Lord, have chosen to abandon their subterranean cities. They seek simpler, more peaceful lives on the surface, hoping to distance themselves from the controversial legacy of their ancestors.Appearance and Identity: A Breaker dwarf's appearance is a testament to their heritage and beliefs. Their pale complexion is often offset by hair shades ranging from rich bronze to fiery red. The red tattoos adorning their bodies are not mere decorations; they are symbols of status, experience, and in many cases, allegiance to the Tyrant Lord.

species.diet: Omnivore with dirt & soil as spices
species.feature_effect: You have a Breaker Pistol. 1 hand, medium range, [[1d4]] piercing damage and automatically hits without a check.

species.name: Flamebeard

description.physical: Flamebeard Dwarves are a fiery and enigmatic subrace, born deep within the volcanic regions of the Dwelfmire. Their molten environment has forged them into a unique people, with skin often tinged with an ashen hue and hair resembling living flames. As they age, some Flamebeards even display molten veins glowing beneath their skin. They are known for their affinity with heat and fire, and their ability to craft weapons infused with elemental energy is legendary. Flamebeards possess a fiery temperament to match their environment, but their passion and loyalty make them invaluable allies in times of need.

species.diet: Omnivore with dirt & soil as spices
species.feature_effect: Once per day, As an action gain immunity to fire damage and your melee weapons deal an additional +3 fire damage for 1 minute

species.name: Hill

description.physical: Hill Dwarves are proud surface-dwellers with sun-bronzed skin and soot-streaked hands. Spending much of their time under the open sky, they are known for their dark, sun-kissed skin and thick black hair, often tied back or braided. Hill Dwarves are practical and hardworking people, skilled in engineering, stone-cutting, and brewing. Despite spending less time underground, they retain a strong connection to their Dwarven heritage and bring their unyielding determination to all aspects of their lives.

species.diet: Omnivore with dirt & soil as spices
species.feature_effect: While wearing armor you have +10 movement speed, you repair your armor to the maximum while taking a long rest

species.name: Mountain

description.physical: Mountain Dwarves are the traditionalists of their kind, born and raised in the massive peaks and deep caverns beneath the surface. With light skin tones and hair ranging from blonde to rich browns, Mountain Dwarves are natural warriors and master craftsmen. They are deeply tied to their subterranean homes, where they mine precious gems and metals and create artifacts of unmatched durability. Mountain Dwarves have a reputation for being stoic, disciplined, and fiercely loyal to their clans. Their skills in forging and their resistance to toxins make them valuable allies and formidable foes.

species.diet: Omnivore with dirt & soil as spices
species.feature_effect: Once per day during a long rest, you can instantly repair one piece of gear like armor or a weapon

species.name: Breaker

description.physical: Long ago, the ancestors of the Breaker Elves sought refuge in the underground realms to escape a cataclysmic event on the surface. Over the centuries, their physiology adapted to the darkness, resulting in the evolution of their distinctive skin tone and hair. Born in the depths and bred for endurance, Breaker Elves are not delicate or free. They were shaped by centuries of indentured servitude beneath the breaker dwarves, pressed into labor, pit fighting, and enforcement. Over time, their bodies adapted, becoming bulkier than surface kin, their skin bears hues of mauve, clay red, and bruised pinks, blending with rusted stone and torchlight. Their hair, always blood-red, is braided or shorn tight for utility, not beauty. Their eyes are adapted to see in the infrared spectrum, allowing them to perceive temperature differences in their environment, which can be especially handy in detecting living beings or working machines

species.feature_effect: When you hit a medium sized creature with a melee attack, you may use an off-action to drive them into the ground or into a wall. The target must succeed a Physical check or be knocked Prone and Pinned until the end of their next turn.

species.name: Duskwither

description.physical: Duskwither Elves thrive where others falter, in deep caves, shadowed forests, and forgotten fungal groves. Their bodies, often pale or patterned with living lichen, are shaped by symbiosis with the shadowy world around them. Some grow bioluminescent spores along their shoulders. Others have spider-silk white hair and eyes that shimmer in dim light. They walk quietly, feel vibrations beneath their bare feet, and hear the whispers of the spore networks like ancient radio signals. Among themselves, they speak in soft tones or not at all, often communicating through motion, spores, or shared silences. Outsiders find them eerie, but those who earn their trust discover a community bound by empathy, instinct, and ancestral memory.

species.feature_effect: As an action once per day as an Off-Action, expand your senses through the fine spores that drift from your hair and skin. Until the end of your next turn, you are aware of all creatures within Short range, even if they are hidden, obscured, or behind cover. You can choose one creature that must make a Mental check or gain the Coughing condition for 1 minute as spores settle in their lungs.
species.fetDuration: Until the end of their next turn

species.name: Forest

description.physical: Forest Elves consider themselves the truest connection to the original elven lineage, living harmoniously with nature and the Feywild. Their homes are often hidden deep within vast forests, where trees not only provide shelter but act as allies, moving to protect their elven caretakers. Forest Elves are graceful and serene, their songs and rituals strengthening the bond between the natural world and their kind. They are often seen as guardians of nature, defending their homes and wildlife from outside threats with a fierceness that belies their delicate appearance.

species.feature_effect: Once per day, you may step into a living tree and emerge from another tree within Medium range and make an attack on a creature at advantage. Both trees must be at least your size. After doing so, you learn simple secrets from the nearby flora or fauna, like their basic emotions, warnings, or recent disturbances.

species.name: Noble

description.physical: Also known as High Elves, Noble Elves pride themselves on their mastery of magic and their long-standing heritage as rulers and scholars. They once formed grand kingdoms in the Feywild, their courts steeped in arcane tradition and timeless beauty. Though many remain in the Feywild, some Noble Elves have returned to the global material plane, eager to reclaim their dominance over mortal kingdoms. Noble Elves are graceful and regal, often exuding an air of superiority, but their intellect and magical prowess make them valuable allies.

species.feature_effect: Start with an additional +2 Mana. When you cast spells you have a +1 to the check and damage

species.name: Shadow

description.physical: Shadow Elves are enigmatic beings born in the Eclipse, a shadowy realm that lies between worlds. Their smoky, ethereal appearance reflects the nature of their homeland, where light and darkness intertwine. These elves are natural wanderers between dimensions, often acting as guides, spies, or guardians of planar boundaries. Their connection to shadow and their ability to move unseen make them mysterious figures in the eyes of other species groups. Despite their otherworldly demeanor, Shadow Elves are deeply thoughtful and often wrestle with the duality of their existence.

species.feature_effect: As a half-action, gain the Invisible condition while in darkness until the end of your next turn.
species.fetDuration: Until the end of their next turn

species.name: Undine

description.physical: Water Elves are amphibious beings who have adapted to life near and in the water. With gills that allow them to breathe both air and water, they are natural swimmers and divers. These elves often form close-knit communities with aquatic races like merfolk, living in harmony with the seas and rivers they call home. Known for their calm and fluid personalities, Water Elves often carry magical water from their homelands, using it to protect and heal. Their connection to the tides and their mastery of water magic make them invaluable in maritime endeavors.

species.feature_effect: You carry enchanted water from your homeland in a jug, gourd, or vessel. At the start of your turn, the water swirls around you, granting +1 armor (max 5). Once per day, as an action, you may release the water to either heal an ally within reach for [[1d6]] or grant them +3 armor for 1 minute. If your vessel is destroyed, you lose this feature until you return to a source of sacred water.

species.name: Hearthless

description.physical: Hearthless Gnomes are wanderers who no longer seek belonging. Once part of the vibrant Feywild, something in them faded long ago. Now, they drift from place to place, setting up pockets of comfort in ruined homes, dead forests, and forgotten tunnels. They are soft-spoken and strangely serene, often tending to others without emotion or judgment. They don't protect because it’s right. They do it because it’s what they do. Their presence quiets the mind, dulls Fear, and warms the bones, even if nothing behind their eyes reflects it. Wherever they stop feels like home… but only while they're there.

species.feature_effect: When you and your party Press On, give everyone a +2 to anything they recover or repair. Additionally, when you stop somewhere you are able to create a comfortable environment. Increased rest healing and safety, it's unlikely that creatures will invade your comfortable space, and if they do it's unlikely that they will attack.

species.name: Tinker

description.physical: The banishment from the fey never disturbed their work. They continue with experiments that have helped and hurt creatures all throughout the world. Their homelands are often sprawling cities of moving parts, practically living traps.

species.feature_effect: You start with three small inventions. The Impossible Pocket is a pocket 6 inches wide that holds up to 100 lbs or 10 bulk. The Light Switch is a small device that toggles a light source on or off within short range as an action. Tikky Tim is a clockwork companion (PL 1) that can be summoned as an action for 1 minute to perform basic tasks.

species.name: Desert

description.physical: These desert orcs used stone moving magics and hard work to create stone dome structures in which they would live as large packs within. However there was constant fighting and the leader of the tribe would need to fight everyone to calm them down, thus how leadership was attained. Desert Orcs prefer to fight in large numbers with several creatures attacking at once

species.feature_effect: Gain +2 to Unarmed Attack checks and damage.

species.name: Jungle

description.physical:
The Green Jungle Orc is the most prevalent Orc option throughout the world. These tribes originally roamed and controlled the Orclands, being the first to develop a written language. Starting with knotted threads, their language evolved into scarification and tattoos, used for tracking time, marking landmarks, and agricultural purposes.
Due to their diet of superfoods, Jungle Orcs grew to impressive sizes and developed the ability to shrug off pain and maintain limitless reserves of energy. These traits allowed them to build an empire based on strength, stamina, and strategic thinking. They are fierce warriors and skilled strategists who once controlled vast portions of the Orclands.

species.feature_effect: If you move toward a creature within 20 ft and make an attack, gain advantage on the attack.

species.name: Maldios

description.physical:
The orcs are not untouched by the corruption of the Vozian flame. In truth, they may have been its first victims. Orc legends whisper that the first orc born became Maldios, a war-driven soul who descended into the burning depths of Ordealis and thrived. There, among the rivers of blood and sandstorms of ash, he rose not just as a survivor, but as a conqueror. Now known as a greater demon of war, Maldios stands over twenty feet tall, his wings scorched and leathery, his armor built from the bones of Rotalisks; terrifying creatures that devour demons. Maldios is wrath embodied, a being of perfect violence and control.
Those orcs who carry Maldios’ blood or have been tainted by his legacy are unmistakable. Their skin burns a bright infernal orange, and their eyes glisten with an internal fire that never dims. They are not savages however, they are tacticians bred in flame. Every movement is calculated. Every reaction is deadly. They do not hesitate. They strike back.

species.feature_effect: As an Off-Action when being hit with a melee attack, you may unleash the stored heat beneath your skin. The creature that hit you takes [[1d4]] Vozian damage. If they are celestial or holy in some way, they instead take [[1d6]] and have disadvantage on their next attack.

species.name: Highstone

description.physical: In the mountains of the Orclands, the pale orcs reign supreme. They possess a mastery of stone and metal that rivals even the dwarves. These rare orcs are not often seen outside of their mountain temples and little is known about their culture or society. They are known for their impressive architectural feats and their skill in working with stone and metal is said to be unmatched. When these orcs leave their lands they are bound magically to secrecy by powerful tattoos.

species.feature_effect: Gain +2 to Attack Checks and Damage with weapons.

species.name: Oceanic

description.physical: Oceanic Orcs are a rugged seafaring culture born in the archipelagos and reef-spined coastlines of the world. Their skin ranges from deep slate to sea-glass green, often mottled with barnacle-like textures or salt-etched markings. Their long limbs, powerful lungs, and strong backs make them natural divers, pearl harvesters, reef scouts, and wave-splitters.Though often mistaken for solitary wanderers, Oceanic Orcs live by a deep code of tidal kinship. Family is fluid but sacred, and every crewmember is a sibling once the salt has bonded them. Their lives are shaped by the rhythm of waves, storm seasons, and the calls of massive sea beasts. They hunt leviathans, ride ocean currents, and sing old songs to calm the waters.Oceanic Orcs are known for their wave-bound adaptability, moving with purpose both above and below water, and for forging gear from coral, whale bone, or shipwreck iron
